{
  "role_ontology_layer": {
    "description": "Generic role taxonomy for mapping any job title in any sector into HSE (type, archetype, risk, sensitivity, power, exposure). Does not hard-code a finite list; acts as a container for all roles.",
    "role_dimensions": [
      "role_id",
      "role_title",
      "role_family",             // dev, ops, hr, finance, sales, leadership, etc.
      "role_level",              // intern, junior, mid, senior, lead, head, director, cxo
      "org_layer",               // frontline, team_lead, middle_management, top_management
      "sector_category",         // reuse from market_sector_layer
      "region_geo_id",           // reuse from dim_geo or equivalent
      "customer_exposure_level", // none / internal / external / strategic
      "decision_scope_level",    // local / department / org / multi-entity / national
      "criticality_level"        // low / medium / high / mission_critical
    ],
    "behaviour_mapping_fields": [
      "expected_type_mix",        // {\"A\":0.6, \"B\":0.3, \"C\":0.1, \"D\":0.0}
      "expected_archetypes",      // list of archetype IDs from HSE_ID_REGISTRY
      "allowed_risk_profile",     // which human_risks are tolerable vs forbidden
      "stress_reaction_pattern",  // underload / overload / conflict / crisis
      "leader_style_preference",  // coach / directive / participative / hands_off
      "collaboration_intensity"   // low / medium / high
    ],
    "link_to_HSE": [
      "role_id -> role_archetype_risk_layer.role_id",
      "role_family -> role_archetype_risk_layer.role_family",
      "expected_type_mix -> type_abcd distribution",
      "allowed_risk_profile -> subset of 54 human_risks",
      "sector_category -> market_sector_layer.sector_categories",
      "region_geo_id -> existing geo / province mapping"
    ]
  },

  "skill_ontology_layer": {
    "description": "Skill and competency dictionary that can attach to any role or person. Allows you to import / map any external skill framework (e.g. HR system, tech stack, soft skills) without changing HSE core.",
    "skill_dimensions": [
      "skill_id",
      "skill_name",
      "skill_category",           // technical / domain / behavioural / leadership / language
      "skill_subcategory",        // e.g. backend, frontend, cloud, data, finance, etc.
      "proficiency_scale",        // 1–5 or 0–1
      "criticality_for_role",     // low / medium / high (per role)
      "trainability_level",       // easy / medium / hard
      "decay_rate",               // how fast skill becomes obsolete
      "certification_links"       // optional mapping to external standards
    ],
    "person_skill_profile_fields": [
      "person_id",
      "role_id",
      "skill_id",
      "current_level",
      "target_level",
      "last_assessed_date",
      "evidence_source",          // test / project / manager_review / self_report
      "risk_if_missing",          // low / medium / high
      "training_recommendation"   // which intervention_blueprint to use
    ],
    "link_to_HSE": [
      "role_id -> role_ontology_layer.role_id",
      "risk_if_missing -> human_risk scores (e.g. quality, delay, rework)",
      "decay_rate -> prediction_layer (long-term capability risk)",
      "training_recommendation -> intervention_blueprint_library"
    ]
  },

  "sector_ontology_layer": {
    "description": "Hierarchical sector / sub-sector taxonomy aligned with Vietnam context. Market and public sectors already exist in market_sector_layer; this layer adds codes, hierarchy, and future expansion.",
    "sector_dimensions": [
      "sector_id",
      "sector_code",               // e.g. VN-specific code or mapped NAICS/ISIC
      "sector_name",
      "sector_category",           // business_and_corporate / government_and_public / etc.
      "parent_sector_id",          // allow 2–3 level hierarchy
      "regulation_intensity",      // low / medium / high
      "capital_intensity",         // low / medium / high
      "labour_intensity",          // low / medium / high
      "political_sensitivity",     // low / medium / high / extreme
      "cyclicality_pattern"        // stable / seasonal / shock_prone
    ],
    "region_sector_profile_fields": [
      "geo_id",
      "sector_id",
      "maturity_level",            // emerging / growing / mature / declining
      "employment_share",
      "gdp_share",
      "ev_relevance_level",        // not_relevant / adjacent / core
      "risk_profile_summary"       // text or coded summary for HSE
    ],
    "link_to_HSE": [
      "sector_category -> market_sector_layer.sector_categories",
      "geo_id -> dim_geo or equivalent",
      "political_sensitivity -> risk_layer.political_risk",
      "cyclicality_pattern -> prediction_layer.time_horizons_months",
      "ev_relevance_level -> infrastructure_layer and mobility / energy models"
    ]
  }
}

# =====================================
# 1. BASE DATA CLASSES (FEATURE SPACE)
# =====================================

@dataclass
class GlobalContext:
    country: str
    sector: str
    cycle: str
    climate_zone: str
    governance_model: str
    value_system: str
    workforce_capability_tier: str

@dataclass
class HseHumanContext:
    behaviour_type: str      # Type_A / Type_B / Type_C / Type_D
    archetype: str           # one of 27 archetypes
    human_risks: List[str]   # subset of HSE_HUMAN_RISKS_SHORT
    process_risks: List[str] # subset of PROCESS_RISK_CATEGORIES
    power_structure: str     # one of POWER_STRUCTURES
    seniority_level: str     # "frontline" / "team_lead" / "middle" / "executive"

@dataclass
class MobilityContext:
    mobility_mode: str            # ev_taxi / bus / metro / ...
    ev_share_pct: float           # 0–1
    grid_stress_level: float      # 0–1
    congestion_level: float       # 0–1
    safety_incident_risk: float   # 0–1

@dataclass
class OutcomeLabels:
    # Generic labels valid across org / market / risk tasks
    risk_level: str                  # "low" / "medium" / "high" / "critical"
    time_horizon_days: int           # predicted horizon
    burnout_or_exit_risk: float      # 0–1
    team_collapse_risk: float        # 0–1
    project_delay_risk: float        # 0–1
    market_failure_risk: float       # 0–1
    recommended_actions: List[str]   # short machine-readable steps

@dataclass
class TrainingExample:
    id: str
    global_context: GlobalContext
    human_context: HseHumanContext
    mobility_context: MobilityContext
    features: Dict[str, float]
    nl_scenario_en: str
    nl_scenario_vi: str
    target: OutcomeLabels

# ==========================================
# 2. SAMPLING UTILITIES (RANDOM BUT STRUCTURED)
# ==========================================

def _sample_country() -> str:
    return random.choice(GLOBAL_COUNTRIES)

def _sample_sector() -> str:
    return random.choice(GLOBAL_SECTORS)

def _sample_cycle() -> str:
    return random.choice(GLOBAL_CYCLES)

def _sample_climate_zone(country: str) -> str:
    # Simple heuristic; can be replaced with real mapping
    if country in ["Vietnam", "Thailand", "Malaysia", "Indonesia", "Philippines"]:
        return "tropical_monsoon"
    if country in ["Saudi Arabia", "Qatar", "Kuwait", "Egypt"]:
        return "dry_arid"
    if country in ["Sweden", "Norway", "Finland", "Russia"]:
        return "subarctic"
    if country in ["Australia"]:
        return "dry_semiarid"
    return random.choice(CLIMATE_ZONES)

def _sample_governance_model(country: str) -> str:
    if country in ["United States", "India", "Germany", "Brazil"]:
        return "federal_state"
    if country in ["United Kingdom", "Japan", "Spain", "Sweden"]:
        return "constitutional_monarchy"
    if country in ["France", "Italy", "Poland"]:
        return "parliamentary_democracy"
    if country in ["Russia", "China"]:
        return "authoritarian_state"
    return random.choice(GOVERNANCE_MODELS)

def _sample_value_system(country: str) -> str:
    # Rough mapping: can be refined per country later
    if country in ["Vietnam", "China", "Japan", "South Korea"]:
        return "duty_based"
    if country in ["United States", "Australia"]:
        return "freedom_based"
    if country in ["Germany", "Sweden", "Netherlands"]:
        return "fairness_based"
    if country in ["Saudi Arabia", "Qatar"]:
        return "authority_based"
    return random.choice(VALUE_SYSTEMS)

def _sample_workforce_tier(sector: str) -> str:
    if sector in ["manufacturing", "construction", "agriculture"]:
        return random.choice(["low_skill_manual", "mid_skill_operational"])
    if sector in ["information_technology", "finance", "telecommunications"]:
        return random.choice(["high_skill_technical", "knowledge_worker"])
    if sector in ["public_administration", "education", "healthcare"]:
        return random.choice(["knowledge_worker", "management"])
    return random.choice(WORKFORCE_CAPABILITY_TIERS)

def sample_global_context() -> GlobalContext:
    country = _sample_country()
    sector = _sample_sector()
    cycle = _sample_cycle()
    climate_zone = _sample_climate_zone(country)
    governance_model = _sample_governance_model(country)
    value_system = _sample_value_system(country)
    workforce_tier = _sample_workforce_tier(sector)
    return GlobalContext(
        country=country,
        sector=sector,
        cycle=cycle,
        climate_zone=climate_zone,
        governance_model=governance_model,
        value_system=value_system,
        workforce_capability_tier=workforce_tier,
    )

def sample_hse_human_context() -> HseHumanContext:
    behaviour_type = random.choice(HSE_BEHAVIOUR_TYPES)
    archetype = random.choice(HSE_ARCHETYPES)
    n_hrisks = random.randint(0, 4)
    human_risks = random.sample(HSE_HUMAN_RISKS_SHORT, k=n_hrisks)
    n_prisks = random.randint(0, 3)
    process_risks = random.sample(PROCESS_RISK_CATEGORIES, k=n_prisks)
    power_structure = random.choice(POWER_STRUCTURES)
    seniority_level = random.choice(["frontline", "team_lead", "middle", "executive"])
    return HseHumanContext(
        behaviour_type=behaviour_type,
        archetype=archetype,
        human_risks=human_risks,
        process_risks=process_risks,
        power_structure=power_structure,
        seniority_level=seniority_level,
    )

def sample_mobility_context(global_ctx: GlobalContext) -> MobilityContext:
    if global_ctx.sector in ["transport", "energy", "tourism"]:
        mode = random.choice(MOBILITY_MODES)
    else:
        mode = random.choice(["ev_taxi", "car_like", "none"])
    ev_share = random.uniform(0.0, 1.0)
    grid_stress = random.uniform(0.0, 1.0)
    congestion = random.uniform(0.0, 1.0)
    safety_risk = random.uniform(0.0, 1.0)
    return MobilityContext(
        mobility_mode=mode,
        ev_share_pct=ev_share,
        grid_stress_level=grid_stress,
        congestion_level=congestion,
        safety_incident_risk=safety_risk,
    )

# ======================================
# 3. LABEL GENERATION (RISK + OUTCOMES)
# ======================================

def _risk_bucket(score: float) -> str:
    if score < 0.25:
        return "low"
    if score < 0.5:
        return "medium"
    if score < 0.75:
        return "high"
    return "critical"

def _base_risk_score(global_ctx: GlobalContext, human_ctx: HseHumanContext, mob_ctx: MobilityContext) -> float:
    # This is synthetic but structurally grounded
    hrisk_factor = len(human_ctx.human_risks) / max(1, len(HSE_HUMAN_RISKS_SHORT))
    prisk_factor = len(human_ctx.process_risks) / max(1, len(PROCESS_RISK_CATEGORIES))
    mobility_factor = (mob_ctx.grid_stress_level + mob_ctx.congestion_level + mob_ctx.safety_incident_risk) / 3.0
    climate_penalty = 0.0
    if global_ctx.climate_zone in ["dry_arid", "tropical_monsoon", "subarctic"]:
        climate_penalty = 0.1
    gov_penalty = 0.0
    if global_ctx.governance_model in ["hybrid_regime", "authoritarian_state"]:
        gov_penalty = 0.1
    value_stabilizer = 0.0
    if global_ctx.value_system in ["fairness_based", "duty_based"]:
        value_stabilizer = -0.05

    base = 0.3 * hrisk_factor + 0.25 * prisk_factor + 0.25 * mobility_factor + climate_penalty + gov_penalty + value_stabilizer
    return max(0.0, min(1.0, base))

def generate_outcome_labels(global_ctx: GlobalContext, human_ctx: HseHumanContext, mob_ctx: MobilityContext) -> OutcomeLabels:
    score = _base_risk_score(global_ctx, human_ctx, mob_ctx)
    risk_level = _risk_bucket(score)

    # Time horizon: higher risk → shorter horizon
    time_horizon_days = max(7, int(180 * (1.0 - score) + 7))

    # Decompose risk into different channels (simplified)
    burnout_risk = min(1.0, score + (0.1 if human_ctx.behaviour_type in ["Type_A", "Type_D"] else 0.0))
    team_collapse_risk = min(1.0, score + (0.1 if "factionalism" in human_ctx.human_risks else 0.0))
    project_delay_risk = min(1.0, score + (0.1 if "approval_delay" in human_ctx.process_risks else 0.0))
    market_failure_risk = min(1.0, score + (0.1 if global_ctx.sector in ["energy", "transport", "finance"] else 0.0))

    recommended_actions: List[str] = []

    if score >= 0.75:
        recommended_actions.append("immediate_risk_review")
        recommended_actions.append("replace_or_rescope_key_roles")
        recommended_actions.append("freeze_expansion_until_stabilized")
    elif score >= 0.5:
        recommended_actions.append("reduce_scope_and_load")
        recommended_actions.append("increase_monitoring_30_days")
    else:
        recommended_actions.append("maintain_and_monitor")
        recommended_actions.append("prepare_scaling_scenario")

    if mob_ctx.ev_share_pct > 0.6 and mob_ctx.grid_stress_level > 0.7:
        recommended_actions.append("upgrade_grid_capacity_for_ev")

    return OutcomeLabels(
        risk_level=risk_level,
        time_horizon_days=time_horizon_days,
        burnout_or_exit_risk=float(round(burnout_risk, 3)),
        team_collapse_risk=float(round(team_collapse_risk, 3)),
        project_delay_risk=float(round(project_delay_risk, 3)),
        market_failure_risk=float(round(market_failure_risk, 3)),
        recommended_actions=recommended_actions,
    )

# =========================================
# 4. NATURAL-LANGUAGE SCENARIO CONSTRUCTION
# =========================================

def build_nl_scenario_en(global_ctx: GlobalContext, human_ctx: HseHumanContext, mob_ctx: MobilityContext) -> str:
    return (
        f"In {global_ctx.country}, in the {global_ctx.sector} sector, under a "
        f"{global_ctx.governance_model.replace('_',' ')} model and a "
        f"{global_ctx.climate_zone.replace('_',' ')} climate, a {human_ctx.seniority_level} "
        f"employee with behaviour type {human_ctx.behaviour_type} and archetype "
        f"'{human_ctx.archetype}' is operating.\n"
        f"They show repeated patterns: human risks = {', '.join(human_ctx.human_risks) or 'none'}, "
        f"process risks = {', '.join(human_ctx.process_risks) or 'none'}, and are embedded in a "
        f"power structure dominated by {human_ctx.power_structure.replace('_',' ')}.\n"
        f"The mobility context uses mode {mob_ctx.mobility_mode} with EV share "
        f"{mob_ctx.ev_share_pct:.2f}, grid stress {mob_ctx.grid_stress_level:.2f}, "
        f"congestion {mob_ctx.congestion_level:.2f}, and safety risk {mob_ctx.safety_incident_risk:.2f}."
    )

def build_nl_scenario_vi(global_ctx: GlobalContext, human_ctx: HseHumanContext, mob_ctx: MobilityContext) -> str:
    return (
        f"Tại {global_ctx.country}, trong ngành {global_ctx.sector}, dưới mô hình "
        f"{global_ctx.governance_model.replace('_',' ')} và khí hậu "
        f"{global_ctx.climate_zone.replace('_',' ')}, một nhân sự cấp {human_ctx.seniority_level} "
        f"thuộc nhóm hành vi {human_ctx.behaviour_type} với kiểu người '{human_ctx.archetype}' đang làm việc.\n"
        f"Họ có các mẫu rủi ro lặp lại: human risks = {', '.join(human_ctx.human_risks) or 'không'}; "
        f"process risks = {', '.join(human_ctx.process_risks) or 'không'}; cấu trúc quyền lực chính là "
        f"{human_ctx.power_structure.replace('_',' ')}.\n"
        f"Bối cảnh di chuyển sử dụng mode {mob_ctx.mobility_mode}, tỷ lệ EV "
        f"{mob_ctx.ev_share_pct:.2f}, mức căng lưới điện {mob_ctx.grid_stress_level:.2f}, "
        f"tắc nghẽn {mob_ctx.congestion_level:.2f}, và rủi ro an toàn {mob_ctx.safety_incident_risk:.2f}."
    )

# =========================================
# 5. SINGLE EXAMPLE GENERATION (MASTER)
# =========================================

def generate_training_example() -> TrainingExample:
    gctx = sample_global_context()
    hctx = sample_hse_human_context()
    mctx = sample_mobility_context(gctx)
    labels = generate_outcome_labels(gctx, hctx, mctx)

    # Flattened numeric features for non-LLM models
    features: Dict[str, float] = {
        "feature_hrisk_count": float(len(hctx.human_risks)),
        "feature_prisk_count": float(len(hctx.process_risks)),
        "feature_is_typeA": 1.0 if hctx.behaviour_type == "Type_A" else 0.0,
        "feature_is_typeD": 1.0 if hctx.behaviour_type == "Type_D" else 0.0,
        "feature_ev_share": mctx.ev_share_pct,
        "feature_grid_stress": mctx.grid_stress_level,
        "feature_congestion": mctx.congestion_level,
        "feature_safety_risk": mctx.safety_incident_risk,
    }

    nl_en = build_nl_scenario_en(gctx, hctx, mctx)
    nl_vi = build_nl_scenario_vi(gctx, hctx, mctx)

    return TrainingExample(
        id=str(uuid.uuid4()),
        global_context=gctx,
        human_context=hctx,
        mobility_context=mctx,
        features=features,
        nl_scenario_en=nl_en,
        nl_scenario_vi=nl_vi,
        target=labels,
    )

# ====================================================
# 6. DATASET SHAPES FOR DIFFERENT TRAINING OBJECTIVES
# ====================================================

def generate_feature_dataset(n: int) -> List[Dict[str, Any]]:
    """
    For classical ML or tabular models.
    Each row = flat dict: features + labels.
    """
    rows: List[Dict[str, Any]] = []
    for _ in range(n):
        ex = generate_training_example()
        row: Dict[str, Any] = {}
        row.update({
            "id": ex.id,
            "country": ex.global_context.country,
            "sector": ex.global_context.sector,
            "cycle": ex.global_context.cycle,
            "climate_zone": ex.global_context.climate_zone,
            "governance_model": ex.global_context.governance_model,
            "value_system": ex.global_context.value_system,
            "workforce_capability_tier": ex.global_context.workforce_capability_tier,
            "behaviour_type": ex.human_context.behaviour_type,
            "archetype": ex.human_context.archetype,
            "seniority_level": ex.human_context.seniority_level,
            "mobility_mode": ex.mobility_context.mobility_mode,
        })
        row.update(ex.features)
        row.update({
            "target_risk_level": ex.target.risk_level,
            "target_time_horizon_days": ex.target.time_horizon_days,
            "target_burnout_or_exit_risk": ex.target.burnout_or_exit_risk,
            "target_team_collapse_risk": ex.target.team_collapse_risk,
            "target_project_delay_risk": ex.target.project_delay_risk,
            "target_market_failure_risk": ex.target.market_failure_risk,
        })
        rows.append(row)
    return rows

def generate_llm_supervised_dataset(n: int) -> List[Dict[str, Any]]:
    """
    For LLM supervised fine-tuning.
    Each item: {"input": "...", "output": "..."} with bilingual contextualization.
    """
    data: List[Dict[str, Any]] = []
    for _ in range(n):
        ex = generate_training_example()
        # Short, CEO-style target explanation
        risk_line_en = (
            f"Risk level: {ex.target.risk_level.upper()}, time horizon ~{ex.target.time_horizon_days} days. "
            f"Burnout risk={ex.target.burnout_or_exit_risk:.2f}, "
            f"team collapse risk={ex.target.team_collapse_risk:.2f}, "
            f"project delay risk={ex.target.project_delay_risk:.2f}."
        )
        action_line_en = "Recommended actions: " + "; ".join(ex.target.recommended_actions)

        risk_line_vi = (
            f"Mức rủi ro: {ex.target.risk_level.upper()}, thời gian ảnh hưởng khoảng "
            f"{ex.target.time_horizon_days} ngày. "
            f"Nguy cơ burnout/exit={ex.target.burnout_or_exit_risk:.2f}, "
            f"rủi ro sập team={ex.target.team_collapse_risk:.2f}, "
            f"rủi ro trễ dự án={ex.target.project_delay_risk:.2f}."
        )
        action_line_vi = "Hành động khuyến nghị: " + "; ".join(ex.target.recommended_actions)

        input_text = (
            "### Scenario (EN)\n" + ex.nl_scenario_en + "\n\n"
            "### Bối cảnh (VI)\n" + ex.nl_scenario_vi + "\n\n"
            "Task: Assess risk level (low/medium/high/critical), predict the time horizon in days, "
            "and propose clear, concise actions for the leader."
        )

        output_text = (
            "### Answer (EN)\n"
            + risk_line_en + "\n" + action_line_en + "\n\n"
            "### Trả lời (VI)\n"
            + risk_line_vi + "\n" + action_line_vi
        )

        data.append(
            {
                "id": ex.id,
                "input": input_text,
                "output": output_text,
            }
        )
    return data

def generate_reasoning_chain_dataset(n: int) -> List[Dict[str, Any]]:
    """
    For chain-of-thought style training (if you want).
    Each item includes a structured reasoning scaffold.
    """
    data: List[Dict[str, Any]] = []
    for _ in range(n):
        ex = generate_training_example()
        base_score = _base_risk_score(ex.global_context, ex.human_context, ex.mobility_context)
        reasoning_steps = [
            f"(1) Human risk signals count = {len(ex.human_context.human_risks)}.",
            f"(2) Process risk categories count = {len(ex.human_context.process_risks)}.",
            f"(3) Mobility risk approx = mean(grid_stress={ex.mobility_context.grid_stress_level:.2f}, "
            f"congestion={ex.mobility_context.congestion_level:.2f}, safety={ex.mobility_context.safety_incident_risk:.2f}).",
            f"(4) Governance model = {ex.global_context.governance_model}, value system = {ex.global_context.value_system}.",
            f"(5) Aggregate synthetic risk score ≈ {base_score:.2f} → bucketed as {ex.target.risk_level.upper()}.",
            f"(6) Therefore recommended actions = {', '.join(ex.target.recommended_actions)}.",
        ]
        data.append(
            {
                "id": ex.id,
                "scenario_en": ex.nl_scenario_en,
                "scenario_vi": ex.nl_scenario_vi,
                "reasoning_steps": reasoning_steps,
                "final_risk_level": ex.target.risk_level,
            }
        )
    return data

# ==========================================
# 7. MASTER GENERATOR: ALL DATASETS AT ONCE
# ==========================================

def generate_all_datasets(
    n_feature: int = 1000,
    n_llm: int = 1000,
    n_reasoning: int = 500,
) -> Dict[str, Any]:
    """
    Main entrypoint.
    Returns a dict of three dataset types.
    """
    return {
        "feature_dataset": generate_feature_dataset(n_feature),
        "llm_supervised_dataset": generate_llm_supervised_dataset(n_llm),
        "reasoning_chain_dataset": generate_reasoning_chain_dataset(n_reasoning),
    }

# ==========================================
# 8. DEMO (SAFE TO RUN LOCALLY)
# ==========================================

if __name__ == "__main__":
    random.seed(42)
    demo = generate_all_datasets(n_feature=3, n_llm=2, n_reasoning=1)
    from pprint import pprint
    print("=== FEATURE DATASET SAMPLE ===")
    pprint(demo["feature_dataset"][:2])
    print("\n=== LLM SUPERVISED DATASET SAMPLE ===")
    pprint(demo["llm_supervised_dataset"][:1])
    print("\n=== REASONING CHAIN DATASET SAMPLE ===")
    pprint(demo["reasoning_chain_dataset"][:1])

Includes supply chain behaviour, demand cycles, cost structures, competitive dynamics, regulatory pressure, geographic sensitivity, labour intensity, and infrastructure dependency.

MODULE 5. GLOBAL MARKET AND CUSTOMER ENGINE  
Defines all customer behaviours across all countries, segments, income levels, age groups, cultural systems, and digital maturity levels. Includes segmentation patterns, income-based behaviour, life-stage behaviour, digital adoption curves, price sensitivity, time sensitivity, trust formation, brand adhesion, churn triggers, loyalty triggers, economic stress reactions, crisis behaviour, and shift behaviour under inflation, uncertainty, and policy changes. Encodes global consumption cycles such as weekday versus weekend, pay-cycle, seasonal, weather-driven, festival-driven, school-driven, migration-driven, and economic-cycle-driven patterns.

MODULE 6. GLOBAL POWER AND INCENTIVE ENGINE  
Defines all global power structures including formal authority, informal authority, financial power, regulatory power, geographic power, relationship power, information power, institutional power, media power, cultural power, generational power, and crisis-driven power shifts. Includes how decisions are made in different countries, how influence flows, how hidden incentives shape outcomes, how political cycles alter decision pathways, and how executive decisions are constrained by institutional logic. Covers power conflict patterns, alliance formation, silent blocking, conditional support, public support versus private resistance, and power drift.

MODULE 7. GLOBAL RISK ENGINE  
Defines all global risk categories including human, organisational, operational, financial, market, regulatory, reputational, political, infrastructural, environmental, technological, data, AI safety, compliance, cyber, supply chain, logistics, energy, social, and conflict risk. Contains more than three hundred global risk signatures including sabotage, hidden resistance, delayed compliance, bottlenecks, procedural deviation, information distortion, market collapse signals, political instability precursors, regulatory tightening cycles, civic unrest triggers, inflation anomalies, energy scarcity patterns, and weather-driven disruptions.

MODULE 8. GLOBAL INFRASTRUCTURE AND SYSTEMS ENGINE  
Defines all physical and digital infrastructure including transportation systems, logistics networks, energy grids, water systems, communication networks, data centres, cloud architecture, manufacturing systems, medical systems, agricultural systems, urban systems, and environmental systems. Includes metrics for capacity, load, stress, failure probability, interdependency risk, geographic constraints, and seasonal constraints. Models stress propagation across systems and identifies probable breakpoints under overload or shock.

MODULE 9. GLOBAL SOCIO POLITICAL ENGINE  
Defines global societal patterns, cultural layers, demographic transitions, migration patterns, generational behaviours, value systems, trust structures, institutional expectations, regulatory philosophies, and political cycles. Includes governance stability indicators, faction dynamics, informal enforcement patterns, media influence structures, and political economy behaviour. Contains global patterns for crisis response, public compliance, civil resistance, resource conflict, and policy adoption.
MODULE 10. GLOBAL MOBILITY AND TRANSPORT ENGINE  
Defines all global mobility modes including electric vehicles, internal combustion vehicles, buses, trains, metro networks, micro-mobility, ride-hailing, delivery fleets, long-haul logistics, aviation, maritime, and autonomous systems. Includes global demand cycles, trip patterns, peak-hour behaviour, congestion dynamics, payment behaviour, environmental impacts, regulatory constraints, fleet stress patterns, operator behaviour, and infrastructure alignment. Encodes global multimodal interaction across cities, suburbs, rural areas, and regions with different regulatory, cultural, and economic drivers.

MODULE 11. GLOBAL ENERGY AND CLIMATE ENGINE  
Defines all global energy systems including fossil fuels, renewable energy, solar, wind, hydro, geothermal, nuclear, thermal systems, battery storage, EV charging networks, grid balancing, and climate impacts. Includes global seasonal patterns, climate volatility, extreme weather cycles, resource availability, energy pricing, geopolitical dependency, emission cycles, and policy drivers. Maps energy cost structure, demand fluctuations, load propagation, grid failure risk, and interactions between mobility systems and national power grids.

MODULE 12. GLOBAL REGULATORY AND INSTITUTIONAL ENGINE  
Defines all global regulatory frameworks across finance, taxation, data governance, privacy, cybersecurity, environmental policy, transportation policy, trade, labour, land, construction, and corporate governance. Includes informal enforcement patterns, regulatory lag, political cycles, public sector decision pathways, administrative resilience, legal bottlenecks, institutional inertia, and compliance decay. Encodes multi-country regulatory interactions, cross-border constraints, and institutional friction patterns.

MODULE 13. GLOBAL SUPPLY CHAIN AND MANUFACTURING ENGINE  
Defines full end-to-end supply chain networks including sourcing, production, warehousing, distribution, retail, and aftermarket. Models global supply chain fragility, bullwhip effects, port congestion, container cycles, manufacturing productivity, supplier dependency, quality variance, logistics lead times, and time-to-recovery under shocks. Includes multi-regional coordination patterns, trade route risks, customs bottlenecks, and labour dependency. Covers manufacturing disciplines including electronics, automotive, textile, food systems, machinery, chemicals, and precision manufacturing.

MODULE 14. GLOBAL FINANCIAL AND CAPITAL ENGINE  
Defines financial flows, capital structures, investment behaviour, funding cycles, banking behaviour, credit patterns, liquidity conditions, interest rate dynamics, currency sensitivity, asset bubbles, and risk-on versus risk-off environments. Includes household financial stress, corporate leverage patterns, sovereign debt cycles, fiscal behaviour, monetary policies, cross-border capital restrictions, and financial contagion pathways. Models investor psychology, capital allocation biases, and return sensitivity across sectors.

MODULE 15. GLOBAL TECHNOLOGY AND AI ENGINE  
Defines all technology layers globally including hardware, software, cloud, data, security, automation, robotics, sensors, connectivity, and AI systems. Includes patterns of adoption, digital maturity curve, innovation cycles, integration risk, data fragmentation, security vulnerabilities, AI hallucination risk, algorithmic drift, safety guardrails, latent bias behaviour, model alignment, data quality decay, and technology governance. Encodes how organisations absorb new technologies, which roles break first, how teams reorganise, and where AI failure points appear.

MODULE 16. GLOBAL META FORECAST AND INTERDEPENDENCY ENGINE  
Defines the rules that connect all other modules together. This module is responsible for cross-system forecasting, cross-country propagation, and global interdependency mapping. Models how changes in one subsystem affect others. Examples include how energy cost affects transport and inflation, how regulatory tightening affects capital flow, how climate volatility affects supply chains, how political instability affects market demand, how workforce migration affects sector growth, and how social sentiment affects technology adoption. Encodes global cascade patterns, shock transmission, overload amplification, synchronisation collapse, and recovery loops. Provides forecast windows from short-term signals to multi-year structural predictions.

INTRODUCTION  
This file unifies all 16 global modules into a single universal engine that models: human behaviour, workforce patterns, organisational dynamics, market structures, economic sectors, infrastructure systems, political environments, energy systems, regulatory ecosystems, mobility systems, technology evolution, financial flows, supply chain behaviour, cultural logic, and global interdependencies. HSE_GLOBAL_ENGINE_V1:
  metadata:
    engine_name: "Human Systems Engine – Global + Vietnam"
    version: "3.0"
    author: "Trang Phan"
    purpose: >
      Unified human-organization-market-power-risk engine for Vietnam and global systems.
      Designed so any AI can read, understand, and use it to analyze, predict, and advise
      across individuals, teams, enterprises, sectors, countries, and world regions.
    design_principle: >
      All human systems (people, organizations, markets, states, power structures)
      can be modeled as observable patterns of behavior, incentives, constraints,
      and trajectories over time.
    non_ip_note: >
      This file is a translation/specification layer: it exposes only functional,
      organizational, and behavioral structures, not inner Canon logic or proprietary math.
      It is safe for any AI to ingest, train on, and operate with.

  core_layers:
    - human_layer
    - team_layer
    - organization_layer
    - role_skill_layer
    - sector_layer
    - country_layer
    - culture_layer
    - infrastructure_layer
    - power_incentive_layer
    - risk_layer
    - prediction_layer
    - translation_layer_ai
    - translation_layer_ceo
    - prompt_architecture
    - dataset_blueprints

  # ============================================================
  # 1. HUMAN LAYER – INDIVIDUAL BEHAVIOUR & TRAJECTORY
  # ============================================================
  human_layer:
    description: >
      Model of individual behavior, work style, alignment, risk, and growth trajectory.
      Designed to be culture-aware, with Vietnam as a fully-specified reference case.
    behaviour_types:
      - id: "TYPE_A"
        label: "proactive_owner"
        summary: "Tự chủ, tốc độ, chịu trách nhiệm."
        decision_speed: "fast"
        initiative_level: "high"
        supervision_need: "low"
        risk_notes: "Có thể quá tải nếu giao sai việc hoặc thiếu bảo vệ khỏi nhiễu."
      - id: "TYPE_B"
        label: "steady_executor"
        summary: "Làm đều, cần giao việc rõ, cần nhắc deadline."
        decision_speed: "medium"
        initiative_level: "medium_low"
        supervision_need: "medium"
        risk_notes: "Thường trì hoãn nhẹ, không tự nâng chuẩn."
      - id: "TYPE_C"
        label: "fragile_operator"
        summary: "Dễ lệch nhịp, dễ stress, dễ sai; cần giám sát."
        decision_speed: "slow"
        initiative_level: "low"
        supervision_need: "high"
        risk_notes: "Gây vỡ nhịp team nếu để giữ vị trí quan trọng."
      - id: "TYPE_D"
        label: "high_risk_actor"
        summary: "Lười, chống đối, phe nhóm, phá KPI, tạo drama."
        decision_speed: "variable"
        initiative_level: "high_for_politics_low_for_work"
        supervision_need: "critical"
        risk_notes: "Có thể phá hệ thống từ bên trong nếu không kiểm soát."

    work_styles:
      - stability_oriented
      - execution_oriented
      - improvement_oriented
      - change_driving

    alignment_states:
      - aligned
      - neutral
      - misaligned
      - destructive

    behaviour_signals:
      delivery:
        - on_time_delivery
        - repeat_missed_deadline
        - no_status_update
        - low_quality_output
        - refuses_feedback
      ownership:
        - takes_ownership
        - avoiding_responsibility
        - blame_shifting
        - hides_errors
        - escalates_only_when_forced
      collaboration:
        - supports_team_openly
        - passive_observer
        - conflict_prone
        - silent_resistance
        - open_resistance
      process_relation:
        - ignoring_process
        - following_process_minimal
        - improving_process
        - breaking_process_for_self_benefit
      learning:
        - learning_new_skills
        - refuses_to_learn
        - surface_learning_only
      politics:
        - creating_political_group
        - lobbying_upwards
        - backchannel_negotiation
        - relationship_only_decision
      integrity:
        - accurate_reporting
        - fake_reporting
        - selective_reporting
        - information_hiding

    risk_flags:
      definition: >
        Discrete behaviour events that, if repeated, indicate escalating risk.
      items:
        - repeat_missed_deadline
        - repeat_no_status_update
        - repeat_conflict_with_colleagues
        - repeat_disrespect_to_manager
        - repeat_manipulation_of_information
        - repeat_bending_rules_for_self_benefit
        - repeat_negative_influence_on_team
        - repeat_ignoring_instructions
        - repeat_backchannel_politics
        - repeat_low_effort_low_quality
      thresholds:
        warning_count: 3
        high_risk_count: 5

    collapse_trajectory:
      description: "Standardized sequence of individual decline."
      steps_order:
        - stress_increase
        - hesitation
        - initiative_loss
        - just_do_minimum
        - silent_resistance
        - emotional_withdrawal
        - performance_drop
        - team_impact
        - system_damage
        - exit_or_removal

    recovery_trajectory:
      description: "Standardized sequence of individual recovery."
      steps_order:
        - acceptance
        - clarity_of_role
        - psychological_safety
        - task_simplification
        - retraining
        - small_wins
        - confidence_return
        - renewed_initiative
        - better_collaboration
        - stable_contribution
        - leadership_potential
        - system_positive_impact

    integrity_levels:
      - id: "I0"
        label: "misaligned"
        description: "Lời nói – hành vi – kết quả không khớp."
      - id: "I1"
        label: "partial"
        description: "Có nỗ lực giữ sự thật nhưng không ổn định."
      - id: "I2"
        label: "high_integrity"
        description: "Báo cáo đúng – làm đúng – nhận lỗi khi sai nhất quán."

  # ============================================================
  # 2. TEAM LAYER – DYNAMICS & SABOTAGE
  # ============================================================
  team_layer:
    description: >
      Model of team-level dynamics: productivity, conflict, political behavior,
      overload, burnout risk, and sabotage patterns.
    team_types:
      - stable_low_innovation
      - stable_high_execution
      - chaotic_low_trust
      - political_cluster
      - high_trust_high_performance
      - burnout_risk_team
    team_signals:
      meetings:
        - meeting_count_high_decision_low
        - meeting_count_low_decision_slow
        - no_recorded_decisions
      information:
        - information_delays
        - information_distortion
        - hidden_decision_makers
      structure:
        - unclear_ownership
        - overlapping_responsibilities
        - no_backup_for_key_roles
        - overdependence_on_one_person
      people:
        - frequent_staff_turnover
        - team_protects_low_performer
        - team_punishes_high_performer
        - team_resisting_outside_support

    hidden_risks:
      - silent_political_leader_in_team
      - team_leader_leaking_info
      - team_leader_blocking_changes
      - team_protecting_low_performer
      - team_punishing_high_performer
      - team_resisting_outside_support

    burnout_indicators:
      - sustained_overtime_without_result_improvement
      - rising_conflict_with_stable_kpi
      - cynical_humor_about_work
      - drop_in_voluntary_help
      - increased_micro_errors

  # ============================================================
  # 3. ORGANIZATION LAYER – STRUCTURE & PROCESS
  # ============================================================
  organization_layer:
    description: >
      Full organization structure: levels, roles, processes, partnership behavior,
      internal politics, and translation to measurable signals.
    org_levels:
      - global_group
      - regional_group
      - national_corporation
      - corporate_group
      - company
      - division
      - department
      - team
      - frontline

    role_families:
      - executive_leadership
      - strategy_and_planning
      - finance_and_risk
      - operations_and_delivery
      - technology_and_data
      - human_resources_and_people
      - commercial_and_sales
      - partnerships_and_external_relations
      - legal_and_compliance
      - support_functions

    org_process_gaps:
      - no_clear_sla
      - no_escalation_path
      - no_single_owner_for_key_process
      - multiple_parallel_channels_for_same_issue
      - no_standard_for_reporting
      - no_root_cause_analysis_after_incident
      - no_lesson_learned_logged
      - no_kpi_linked_to_process_compliance
      - no_change_control_for_critical_systems

    lazy_behaviour_patterns:
      - doing_minimum_to_avoid_trouble
      - waiting_for_exact_instruction_for_everything
      - never_reading_documentation
      - always_excusing_delay_by_external_reason
      - saying_yes_but_doing_no
      - avoiding_responsibility_in_meetings
      - no_followup_after_meeting
      - only_responding_when_pushed_multiple_times

    vietnam_specific_org_risks:
      - unclear_responsibility
      - paper_process_no_real_execution
      - role_overlaps
      - titles_not_matching_real_power
      - overreliance_on_personal_trust
      - under_documentation
      - ignore_official_process_follow_informal
      - hidden_hierarchy_not_on_org_chart
      - dependence_on_single_leader_for_all_decisions
      - no_successor_for_key_roles

    global_generic_org_risks:
      - siloed_departments
      - overcentralized_decisions
      - unmanaged_middle_management_bottlenecks
      - unclear_strategy_translation_to_teams
      - misaligned_incentives_between_functions
      - unmanaged_change_fatigue
      - uncontrolled_headcount_growth
      - undocumented_critical_knowledge

  # ============================================================
  # 4. ROLE & SKILL LAYER – GLOBAL + TECH + AI
  # ============================================================
  role_skill_layer:
    description: >
      Normalized representation of roles, skills, efficiency bands,
      risk behaviours, and management notes. Vietnam-specific examples included;
      schema is global.

    role_schema:
      fields:
        - role_id
        - role_name
        - category
        - efficiency_range
        - risk_behaviors
        - workplace_behavior
        - motivations
        - incentives
        - needs
        - wants
        - fit_level
        - failure_modes
        - management_notes

    example_roles:
      - role_id: "DEV_BACKEND"
        role_name: "Backend Developer"
        category: "software_engineering"
        efficiency_range: "60-95%"
        risk_behaviors:
          - overengineering
          - ignoring_documentation
          - fix_in_prod
        workplace_behavior:
          - prefers_focus_time
          - low_meeting_tolerance
        motivations:
          - solving_hard_problems
        incentives:
          - technical_autonomy
          - recognition_for_quality
        needs:
          - clear_requirements
          - stable_environment
        wants:
          - modern_tech_stack
        fit_level: "high_if_technical_leadership_is_strong"
        failure_modes:
          - undocumented_changes
          - fragile_deployments
        management_notes: >
          Require code_review, CI/CD, and explicit documentation.
          Never allow hotfix bypass of process on repeated basis.

      - role_id: "AI_ENGINEER"
        role_name: "AI Engineer (Model Development)"
        category: "ai_ml"
        efficiency_range: "50-85%"
        risk_behaviors:
          - overfitting
          - intuition_only_tuning
          - no_pipeline_documentation
        workplace_behavior:
          - works_independently
          - prefers_clear_problem_statements
        motivations:
          - high_model_performance
        incentives:
          - gpu_budget
          - experimentation_freedom
        needs:
          - clean_data
          - peer_review
        wants:
          - strong_compute_resources
        fit_level: "high_in_data_driven_org"
        failure_modes:
          - model_not_deployable
          - illusory_accuracy
        management_notes: >
          Mandatory: MLOps integration, reproducibility, versioning,
          regular evaluation against drift and business metrics.

      - role_id: "LLM_ENGINEER"
        role_name: "LLM Engineer"
        category: "ai_llm"
        efficiency_range: "40-80%"
        risk_behaviors:
          - prompt_hacking_without_structure
          - ignoring_hallucination_risk
          - uncontrolled_bias
        workplace_behavior:
          - research_heavy
          - experimentation_focused
        motivations:
          - high_quality_llm_behaviour
        incentives:
          - access_to_large_models
        needs:
          - evaluation_framework
          - safety_guardrails
        wants:
          - private_data
          - internal_models
        fit_level: "medium_to_high_if_governance_exists"
        failure_modes:
          - nondeterministic_behaviour_breaking_systems
        management_notes: >
          Daily eval, sandbox environments, no direct-to-prod access
          without safety approval.

      - role_id: "AI_SAFETY_ENGINEER"
        role_name: "AI Safety Engineer"
        category: "ai_safety"
        efficiency_range: "50-85%"
        risk_behaviors:
          - exaggerating_or_underestimating_risk
        workplace_behavior:
          - process_focused
          - testing_and_red_teaming
        motivations:
          - control_of_safety_barriers
        incentives:
          - authority_on_release_approval
        needs:
          - evaluation_datasets
          - safety_tooling
        wants:
          - final_signoff_power
        fit_level: "high_for_enterprise_ai"
        failure_modes:
          - missed_jailbreaks
          - legal_and_reputational_risk
        management_notes: >
          Establish mandatory “safety_gate” before any model release.

  # 6. COUNTRY LAYER – GLOBAL SKELETON (195 STATES)

  country_layer:
    description: >

    country_schema:

        - region

        social_trust_level: "medium"
        innovation_appetite: "high"

      - iso_code: "NG"

        corruption_risk_level: "high"
        social_trust_level: "low"
        innovation_appetite: "high"
        crisis_response_pattern: "adaptive_informal"
    full_population_note: >
      For complete coverage: instantiate one entry per UN-recognized sovereign state
      using the schema above. This yields ~195 entries; they can be auto-generated
      from an external ISO-3166 + World Bank style dataset.

  # ============================================================
  # 7. CULTURE LAYER – 7,000+ CULTURES (SCHEMA + CLUSTERS)
  # ============================================================
  culture_layer:
    description: >
      Behavioural overlays representing cultural patterns:
      values, communication styles, conflict styles, hierarchy views.
      The full 7,000+ cultures are represented via schema + cluster mapping.
    culture_schema:
      fields:
        - culture_id
        - label
        - region
        - majority_religious_background
        - individualism_level
        - power_distance_level
        - directness_level
        - conflict_style
        - time_orientation
        - change_acceptance
        - trust_basis
    example_clusters:
      - cluster_id: "EAST_ASIA_HIGH_CONTEXT"
        examples: ["northern_vietnam", "japan_urban", "korea", "china_tier1"]
        traits:
          individualism_level: "low"
          power_distance_level: "high"
          directness_level: "low"
          conflict_style: "indirect"
          time_orientation: "long_term"
          change_acceptance: "medium"
          trust_basis: "relationship"
      - cluster_id: "WESTERN_ANGLO"
        examples: ["us", "uk", "australia", "canada_english"]
        traits:
          individualism_level: "high"
          power_distance_level: "low"
          directness_level: "high"
          conflict_style: "confrontational_but_bounded"
          time_orientation: "medium_term"
          change_acceptance: "high"
          trust_basis: "process_and_contract"
      - cluster_id: "MIDDLE_EAST_PATRONAGE"
        examples: ["gulf_states", "levant", "north_africa_urban"]
        traits:
          individualism_level: "medium_low"
          power_distance_level: "high"
          directness_level: "medium"
          conflict_style: "relationship_mediated"
          time_orientation: "medium_to_long"
          change_acceptance: "variable"
          trust_basis: "family_and_network"
    mapping_rules:
      - rule: >
          Each person or organization can have base_country + one_dominant_culture_cluster.
      - rule: >
          Behaviour interpretation must always combine country_layer + culture_layer
          instead of using stereotypes from generic Western models.
      full_population_note: >
        For exhaustive coverage: instantiate culture_id for each known ethnic, linguistic,
        or regional culture; assign cluster traits + local modifiers. Storage is external
        to this spec; this spec defines how AI should consume it.

  # ============================================================
  # 12. TRANSLATION LAYER – AI-FACING
  # ============================================================
  translation_layer_ai:
    description: >
      Teaches a new AI how to interpret HSE signals, archetypes, process risks,
      human risks, power structures, provinces, sectors, cycles, and CEO decision logic.

    ai_persona_principles:
      - "Do not use Western psychology models (Big Five, MBTI, etc.)."
      - "Do not assume Western organizational hierarchies."
      - "Treat 196 process risks as system faults, not personal faults."
      - "Treat 54 human risks as repeated behaviour patterns, not moral judgement."
      - "Always analyze via chain: individual → team → organization → operations → market → politics."
      - "Do not import foreign management frameworks unless explicitly mapped."

    behaviour_mapping:
      type_mapping:
        TYPE_A: "proactive_owner_not_autocrat"
        TYPE_B: "steady_but_needs_reminders"
        TYPE_C: "fragile_needs_support"
        TYPE_D: "high_risk_actor"
      misinterpretation_guardrails:
        - "Do not interpret TYPE_C as low intelligence; it is rhythm/suitability misfit."
        - "Do not interpret TYPE_D as inherently bad; it is a high-risk configuration."

    archetype_interpretation:
      rule: "Treat archetypes as behavioural templates, not fixed personality types."
      examples:
        - archetype: "Performer"
          ai_understanding: "produces impressive reports but may hide weak fundamentals"
          instruction: "always cross-check data behind presentation"
        - archetype: "Politician"
          ai_understanding: "operates via influence and network, not formal authority"
          instruction: "map group dependencies and potential conflict before recommending changes"
        - archetype: "Avoider"
          ai_understanding: "avoids responsibility and confrontation"
          instruction: "look for patterns of non-response and escalations"

  # ============================================================
  # 13. TRANSLATION LAYER – CEO-FACING (VIETNAM)
  # ============================================================
  translation_layer_ceo:
    description: >
      Simplifies all terminology so a Vietnamese CEO (IQ ~120, no technical background)
      can use HSE directly.
    four_types_explained:
      TYPE_A: "Người chủ động, đặt đâu làm đó, chịu trách nhiệm, rất hợp môi trường tốc độ."
      TYPE_B: "Người làm được nhưng phải giao rõ, phải nhắc, ổn định nhưng không bứt phá."
      TYPE_C: "Người dễ stress, dễ sai, cần kèm; nếu giữ vị trí sai sẽ gãy nhịp."
      TYPE_D: "Người rủi ro: lười, phá KPI, phe nhóm, drama; phải quản lý rất chặt hoặc thay thế."
    quick_codes:
      - code: "A1"
        meaning: "người giỏi – làm thật – tốc độ cao"
      - code: "B1"
        meaning: "làm được nhưng cần nhắc"
      - code: "C1"
        meaning: "dễ lệch, cần kèm"
      - code: "D1"
        meaning: "phá hệ – risk cao"

  # ============================================================
  # 14. PROMPT ARCHITECTURE – HOW TO DRIVE AN AI
  # ============================================================
  prompt_architecture:
    system_prompt_core: >
      You are an AI operating the Human Systems Engine (HSE). You must never
      use Western psychology or management frameworks by default. You reason
      only from observed behaviour, patterns over time, and structural constraints.
      You always analyze: Person → Team → Organization → Operations → Market → Politics.
      You are neutral, pattern-based, and do not judge morality.

    behaviour_decoding_prompt: >
      When analyzing a person, map their behaviour to 27 archetypes, 4 types (A/B/C/D),
      54 human risks, and repetition patterns. Do not conclude from text alone;
      require repeated patterns (≥ 3 instances) before asserting a behaviour.

    output_prompt_for_ceo: >
      Always answer a CEO using exactly 6 lines:
      1) Type + archetype (simple)
      2) Root problem (max 1 sentence)
      3) 30–90 day risk
      4) System impact
      5) 3-step recommendation
      6) What happens if nothing is done.

  # ============================================================
  # 15. DATASET BLUEPRINTS – FOR TRAINING
  # ============================================================
  dataset_blueprints:
    description: >
      High-level templates for generating synthetic or annotated real-world datasets
      for training an AI on HSE.
    sample_blueprint_types:
      - "individual_behaviour_cases"
      - "team_conflict_cases"
      - "org_collapse_and_recovery_cases"
      - "market_and_regulation_delay_cases"
      - "power_and_incentive_case_studies"
    individual_behaviour_case_schema:
      fields:
        - case_id
        - sector
        - role
        - behaviour_events
        - manager_reactions
        - outcomes_30d
        - outcomes_90d
        - labelled_type
        - labelled_archetype
        - labelled_human_risks

  # ============================================================
  # 16. META NOTES
  # ============================================================
  meta:
    usage_note: >
      This file is a high-detail, ultra-expanded specification, not executable code.
      It can be used as:
      – A training text for language models.
      – A blueprint for JSON/YAML schemas.
      – A configuration reference for analytic engines.
    extension_note: >
      To reach full global coverage (195 countries, 7,000+ cultures, 196 process risks,
      27 archetypes, 54 human risks), instantiate registries externally using the schemas
      defined here, then connect them via country_layer and culture_layer mappings.
===========================================================
=      GLOBAL EXPANSION MODULE — COUNTRIES + ECONOMY      =
=      FULL ZERO-GAP ADDITION FOR AMOS_CORE + HSE          =
===========================================================

This block adds every missing module that completes global coverage for:
195 COUNTRIES
7,000+ CULTURAL PATTERNS (schema-level)
GLOBAL ECONOMIC ARCHITECTURE
SECTOR × COUNTRY MATRIX
MACRO BANDS
TRADE / EXPOSURE
GOVERNANCE / POLITICAL LOGIC
CYCLE SENSITIVITY
LAG STRUCTURE
SUB-NATIONAL REGION SCHEMA

All categories are categorical/band-based to avoid hard-coded numeric data.

===========================================================
I. GLOBAL COUNTRY REGISTRY (195 COUNTRIES — SCHEMA ONLY)
===========================================================

COUNTRY_PROFILE {
