HSE_GLOBAL_TRANSLATION_AND_TRAINING_SPEC_V1
==========================================

# PURPOSE
# -------
# This file extends the Vietnam-focused Human Systems Engine (HSE) into a
# GLOBAL ENGINE SHELL that can cover:
# - all countries
# - all sectors and sub-sectors
# - all roles and role-families
# - skills and behavioural overlays
# - global macro & political context
# - automatic training dataset generation for AI models
#
# It is structurally exhaustive (0 structural gap).
# The content values (weights, probabilities, etc.) are intentionally left
# as parameters to be filled from data or expert input.


META
----

engine_name: "HSE_GLOBAL_TRANSLATION_ENGINE"
version: "1.0"
author: "Trang Phan"
scope:
  - unify the Vietnam HSE into a global shell
  - preserve Vietnam as the most detailed implementation
  - allow plug-in of additional countries / regions with correct structure
primary_use_cases:
  - train AI models to read human systems in any country
  - provide globally consistent behavioural & system interpretation
  - keep politics / culture encoded as variables, not narrative
core_principle:
  - any human system (person, team, org, sector, state, civilisation)
    can be represented as:
    (context) → (behaviour patterns) → (system states) → (trajectories)


=========================================================
1. GLOBAL DIMENSION REGISTRIES (MASTER LOOKUP TABLES)
=========================================================

These dimensions are shared globally and referenced everywhere.
They are deliberately modular so Vietnam remains a "hyper-detailed" instance.


1.1 COUNTRY REGISTRY (195+ COUNTRIES)
-------------------------------------

# Each country = one row in this registry.
# NOTE: values like macro_type / governance_style are placeholders to be
# filled from structured sources. The schema is exhaustive.

TABLE dim_country (
  id                INTEGER PK,
  iso2              STRING,       # e.g. "VN", "US"
  iso3              STRING,       # e.g. "VNM", "USA"
  name_en           STRING,       # English name
  name_local        STRING,       # Local language name (optional)
  world_region      STRING,       # "asia", "europe", "africa", "americas", "oceania", "mena", "global"
  income_band       STRING,       # "low", "lower_middle", "upper_middle", "high"
  political_system  STRING,       # "democracy", "single_party", "monarchy", "hybrid", "other"
  governance_style  STRING,       # e.g. "rules_based", "relationship_based", "mixed"
  legal_tradition   STRING,       # "civil_law", "common_law", "mixed", "religious_law"
  labour_model      STRING,       # "formal_heavy", "informal_heavy", "mixed"
  digital_maturity  STRING,       # "low", "emerging", "mature", "advanced"
  social_trust_band STRING,       # "very_low", "low", "medium", "high"
  conflict_risk     STRING,       # "low", "medium", "high", "extreme"
  corruption_band   STRING,       # "low", "medium", "high", "very_high"
  regulatory_speed  STRING,       # "fast", "medium", "slow", "very_slow"
  state_capacity    STRING,       # "weak", "developing", "strong", "very_strong"
  notes             TEXT          # free-form, per-country nuance
)

# EXAMPLES (illustrative, values to be calibrated; VN kept explicit)

dim_country_examples:
  - { iso2: "VN", iso3: "VNM", name_en: "Viet Nam", name_local: "Việt Nam",
      world_region: "asia", income_band: "lower_middle",
      political_system: "single_party", governance_style: "relationship_based",
      legal_tradition: "civil_law", labour_model: "informal_heavy",
      digital_maturity: "emerging", social_trust_band: "medium",
      conflict_risk: "low", corruption_band: "high",
      regulatory_speed: "slow", state_capacity: "developing",
      notes: "HSE-VN is the fully detailed instance." }

  - { iso2: "US", iso3: "USA", name_en: "United States of America", name_local: "",
      world_region: "americas", income_band: "high",
      political_system: "democracy", governance_style: "rules_based",
      legal_tradition: "common_law", labour_model: "formal_heavy",
      digital_maturity: "advanced", social_trust_band: "medium",
      conflict_risk: "low", corruption_band: "medium",
      regulatory_speed: "mixed", state_capacity: "strong",
      notes: "high litigation, sectoral regulation strong." }

  - { iso2: "JP", iso3: "JPN", name_en: "Japan", name_local: "日本",
      world_region: "asia", income_band: "high",
      political_system: "democracy", governance_style: "rules_based",
      legal_tradition: "civil_law", labour_model: "formal_heavy",
      digital_maturity: "advanced", social_trust_band: "high",
      conflict_risk: "low", corruption_band: "low",
      regulatory_speed: "medium", state_capacity: "strong",
      notes: "high group conformity, stability priority." }

  - { iso2: "IN", iso3: "IND", name_en: "India", name_local: "भारत",
      world_region: "asia", income_band: "lower_middle",
      political_system: "democracy", governance_style: "mixed",
      legal_tradition: "common_law", labour_model: "informal_heavy",
      digital_maturity: "emerging", social_trust_band: "medium",
      conflict_risk: "medium", corruption_band: "high",
      regulatory_speed: "slow", state_capacity: "developing",
      notes: "high variance across states." }


1.2 GLOBAL SECTOR REGISTRY
--------------------------

# Extends Vietnam 62 sectors into a global catalogue.

TABLE dim_sector (
  id                 INTEGER PK,
  sector_code        STRING,    # e.g. "banking", "ev_mobility"
  sector_group       STRING,    # "finance", "mobility", "healthcare", "public", "digital", etc.
  description_en     TEXT,
  capital_intensity  STRING,    # "low", "medium", "high"
  regulation_level   STRING,    # "low", "medium", "high"
  labour_intensity   STRING,    # "low", "medium", "high"
  digital_dependency STRING,    # "low", "medium", "high"
  safety_critical    BOOLEAN,
  public_exposure    STRING,    # "low", "medium", "high"
  notes              TEXT
)

# The Vietnam set is a subset of this (ev_mobility, logistics, etc.).
# For global coverage, add: oil_gas, mining, defense, etc.


1.3 ROLE FAMILY REGISTRY (GLOBAL)
---------------------------------

# All roles from VN HSE + global tech/business roles.

TABLE dim_role_family (
  id              INTEGER PK,
  family_code     STRING,   # "engineering_backend", "sales_b2b", "policy_maker"
  family_group    STRING,   # "tech", "ops", "leadership", "governance", "field", "support"
  description_en  TEXT,
  risk_profile    STRING,   # "low", "medium", "high"
  integrity_load  STRING,   # how much system integrity depends on this family
  automation_risk STRING,   # "low", "medium", "high"
  notes           TEXT
)


1.4 ROLE REGISTRY (GLOBAL)
--------------------------

TABLE dim_role (
  id                 INTEGER PK,
  role_code          STRING,   # e.g. "backend_engineer", "ceo", "truck_driver"
  role_name_en       STRING,
  role_name_local    STRING,
  role_family_id     INTEGER FK dim_role_family,
  seniority_band     STRING,   # "junior", "mid", "senior", "lead", "exec"
  remote_feasibility STRING,   # "onsite", "hybrid", "remote_possible", "remote_friendly"
  risk_behaviour_band STRING,  # "low", "medium", "high"
  criticality_band   STRING,   # "low", "medium", "high", "system_core"
  notes              TEXT
)

# All VN tech roles (Dev, Cloud, AI, Security, Ops, etc.) plug here.


1.5 SKILL REGISTRY
------------------

TABLE dim_skill (
  id             INTEGER PK,
  skill_code     STRING,     # "python", "negotiation", "fleet_planning"
  skill_group    STRING,     # "technical", "people", "management", "domain"
  description_en TEXT,
  measurement_method STRING, # "assessment", "kpi", "certification", "peer_review"
  typical_roles  TEXT        # CSV of dim_role.role_code
)


1.6 GLOBAL BEHAVIOUR & ARCHETYPE REGISTRY
-----------------------------------------

# Preserve Type A/B/C/D and 27 archetypes, but allow culture overlays.

TABLE dim_behaviour_archetype (
  id               INTEGER PK,
  archetype_code   STRING,   # "performer", "politician", etc.
  description_en   TEXT,
  base_risk_band   STRING,   # "low", "medium", "high"
  base_type        STRING,   # "A", "B", "C", "D" (dominant)
  system_effect    STRING,   # "stabilize", "accelerate", "fragment", "distort_signal"
  notes            TEXT
)

TABLE dim_behaviour_risk (
  id               INTEGER PK,
  risk_code        STRING,   # "intentional_delay", "fake_report"
  description_en   TEXT,
  category         STRING,   # "delay", "information", "politics", "quality", ...
  structural_root  STRING,   # "process", "incentive", "capacity", "culture"
  severity_band    STRING,   # "low", "medium", "high", "critical"
  notes            TEXT
)


1.7 CULTURE / VALUE SYSTEM REGISTRY (GLOBAL)
-------------------------------------------

# This is the abstraction layer instead of listing 7,000 cultures one-by-one.
# Each real culture maps to a COMPOSITION of these dimensions.

TABLE dim_value_system (
  id                    INTEGER PK,
  code                  STRING,  # e.g. "collectivist_high_power_distance"
  description_en        TEXT,
  individualism_level   STRING,  # "low", "medium", "high"
  power_distance_level  STRING,  # "low", "medium", "high"
  uncertainty_avoidance STRING,  # "low", "medium", "high"
  short_long_term       STRING,  # "short_term", "balanced", "long_term"
  communication_style   STRING,  # "direct", "indirect", "mixed"
  conflict_style        STRING,  # "avoidant", "confrontational", "managed"
  time_orientation      STRING,  # "monochronic", "polychronic"
  notes                 TEXT
)

TABLE dim_culture_profile (
  id               INTEGER PK,
  culture_code     STRING,   # "vn_urban_tech", "us_startup", "jp_corporate", "ke_informal_trading"
  display_name_en  STRING,
  country_id       INTEGER FK dim_country,
  value_system_id  INTEGER FK dim_value_system,
  urban_rural      STRING,   # "urban", "suburban", "rural", "mixed"
  generation_focus STRING,   # "gen_x", "gen_y", "gen_z", "mixed"
  economic_band    STRING,   # "low", "lower_middle", "upper_middle", "high"
  digital_profile  STRING,   # "offline", "hybrid", "online_heavy"
  notes            TEXT
)

# "7,000+ cultures" = 7,000+ rows in dim_culture_profile.
# The spec is exhaustive; the rows are data to be generated/fitted.


1.8 GLOBAL TIME / CYCLE REGISTRY
--------------------------------

TABLE dim_time_cycle_global (
  id               INTEGER PK,
  cycle_code       STRING,   # "day_morning", "weekend", "salary_week", "holiday_peak"
  cycle_type       STRING,   # "day", "week", "month", "season", "event"
  description_en   TEXT,
  macro_relevance  STRING,   # "low", "medium", "high"
  behavioural_load STRING,   # "low", "medium", "high"
  notes            TEXT
)

# Vietnam time cycles (Tet, flood season, etc.) map here with country_id = VN
# Others: Ramadan, Golden Week, Thanksgiving, etc.


=========================================================
2. CONTEXT-TO-BEHAVIOUR MAPPING (GLOBAL SHELL)
=========================================================

This is where country + culture + sector + role → behaves like what?


2.1 GLOBAL HUMAN CONTEXT
------------------------

STRUCT human_context_global:
  country_id        INT
  culture_profile_id INT
  sector_id         INT
  role_id           INT
  time_cycle_id     INT
  org_size_band     STRING   # "micro", "sme", "large", "mega"
  org_formality     STRING   # "informal", "semi_formal", "formal"
  job_security_band STRING   # "low", "medium", "high"
  enforcement_level STRING   # "weak", "medium", "strong" (rules enforcement)
  compensation_band STRING   # "low", "market", "premium"
  stress_band       STRING   # "low", "medium", "high"
  integrity_culture STRING   # "weak", "mixed", "strong"
  notes             TEXT


2.2 CONTEXT → BEHAVIOUR RULES
------------------------------

TABLE fact_behaviour_pattern_global (
  id                   INTEGER PK,
  country_id           INTEGER FK dim_country,
  culture_profile_id   INTEGER FK dim_culture_profile,
  sector_id            INTEGER FK dim_sector,
  role_family_id       INTEGER FK dim_role_family,
  role_id              INTEGER FK dim_role,
  time_cycle_id        INTEGER FK dim_time_cycle_global,

  # Output bands (probabilities / likelihoods)
  prob_type_A          NUMERIC,  # 0–1
  prob_type_B          NUMERIC,
  prob_type_C          NUMERIC,
  prob_type_D          NUMERIC,

  prob_archetype_ids   TEXT,     # JSON array of (archetype_id → weight)
  prob_human_risk_ids  TEXT,     # JSON array of (risk_id → probability)
  burnout_risk_band    STRING,   # "low", "medium", "high", "critical"
  churn_risk_band      STRING,
  political_risk_band  STRING,   # risk of internal group politics

  # System effects
  system_stability_effect STRING, # "stabilizing", "neutral", "destabilizing"
  execution_speed_effect  STRING, # "fast", "balanced", "slow"
  transparency_effect     STRING, # "increase", "neutral", "decrease"
  notes                TEXT
)

# Vietnam-specific HSE rules are a dense, high-resolution subset where country_id = VN.


2.3 PROCESS RISK: GLOBAL SHELL
------------------------------

TABLE fact_process_risk_global (
  id                 INTEGER PK,
  country_id         INTEGER FK dim_country,
  sector_id          INTEGER FK dim_sector,
  org_size_band      STRING,
  cycle_id           INTEGER FK dim_time_cycle_global,

  process_risk_code  STRING,     # reused from VN 196 codes + global additions
  category           STRING,     # "alignment", "approval", "ops", "tech", "market", "politics", "infra"
  severity_band      STRING,     # "low", "medium", "high", "critical"
  likelihood_band    STRING,     # "low", "medium", "high"
  system_level       STRING,     # "team", "department", "org", "sector", "national"
  notes              TEXT
)


2.4 POWER & INCENTIVE: GLOBAL
-----------------------------

TABLE dim_power_structure_global (
  id                 INTEGER PK,
  power_code         STRING,   # "formal_title", "informal_decider", "clan_power", ...
  description_en     TEXT,
  visibility         STRING,   # "visible", "semi_hidden", "hidden"
  stability          STRING,   # "short_term", "medium_term", "long_term"
  risk_band          STRING,   # "low", "medium", "high"
  notes              TEXT
)

TABLE fact_power_mapping_global (
  id                 INTEGER PK,
  country_id         INTEGER FK dim_country,
  sector_id          INTEGER FK dim_sector,
  org_size_band      STRING,
  power_code         STRING FK dim_power_structure_global,
  typical_entry_point STRING,  # "board", "regulator", "middle_management", "local_admin"
  typical_effect      STRING,  # "accelerate", "block", "distort", "redistribute_benefit"
  notes              TEXT
)


=========================================================
3. TRAINING DATASET GENERATION LAYER
=========================================================

This layer explains how to automatically synthesize training examples for AI.


3.1 SYNTHETIC CASE GENERATOR SPEC
---------------------------------

STRUCT training_case_global:
  id                    STRING,   # unique case ID
  country_iso2          STRING,
  sector_code           STRING,
  role_code             STRING,
  culture_code          STRING,
  time_cycle_code       STRING,

  # Human-level features
  behaviour_observed    TEXT,     # short narrative of what is seen
  pattern_flags         TEXT,     # JSON: { "DELAY_INTENT": true, ... }
  type_estimate         STRING,   # "A", "B", "C", or "D"
  archetype_estimate    TEXT,     # e.g. "politician+performer"
  human_risk_list       TEXT,     # list of risk codes

  # System-level features
  process_risks         TEXT,     # list of process_risk_code
  power_structures      TEXT,     # power codes present
  system_state_summary  TEXT,     # "info_bottleneck_at_middle_management", etc.
  time_horizon_months   INTEGER,  # 1, 3, 6, 12, 24, 36
  outcome_label         TEXT,     # "team_collapse", "stable_growth", etc.

  # Target outputs for supervised training
  target_explanation    TEXT,     # explanation in structured CEO language
  target_recommendation TEXT,     # recommended 3–5 actions
  target_risk_score     NUMERIC,  # 0–1 risk of collapse/major issue
  target_confidence     NUMERIC   # 0–1 confidence of label


3.2 GLOBAL DATASET GENERATION RECIPES
-------------------------------------

TABLE dataset_recipe_global (
  id                 INTEGER PK,
  name               STRING,  # "vn_ceo_training_set", "us_startup_pm_set"
  description_en     TEXT,
  target_country_ids TEXT,    # JSON array
  target_sector_ids  TEXT,    # JSON array
  target_roles       TEXT,    # JSON array of role_codes
  sample_size        INTEGER,
  balance_strategy   STRING,  # "balanced_by_risk", "reflect_real_distribution"
  label_strategy     STRING,  # "expert_curated", "rule_based", "mixed"
  notes              TEXT
)

# A generator process (outside this spec) reads dataset_recipe_global,
# samples from dim_* + fact_* tables, and emits training_case_global rows.


=========================================================
4. LANGUAGE TRANSLATION LAYER (EN ↔ LOCAL)
=========================================================

Goal: keep professional, neutral tone, avoid cultural bias.


4.1 TERM MAPPING REGISTRY
-------------------------

TABLE dim_term_mapping (
  id                 INTEGER PK,
  concept_code       STRING,    # "intentional_delay", "fake_report", ...
  terminology_domain STRING,    # "behaviour", "process_risk", "power", ...
  lang_code          STRING,    # "en", "vi", "jp", "fr", ...
  label              STRING,    # translated label
  description        TEXT       # clear explanation in that language
)

# Example entries (for EN and VI):

dim_term_mapping_examples:
  - { concept_code: "intentional_delay",
      terminology_domain: "behaviour",
      lang_code: "en",
      label: "Intentional delay",
      description: "Delays tasks on purpose to avoid responsibility or to gain leverage." }

  - { concept_code: "intentional_delay",
      terminology_domain: "behaviour",
      lang_code: "vi",
      label: "Trì hoãn có chủ đích",
      description: "Cố tình kéo dài thời gian xử lý công việc để né trách nhiệm hoặc tạo lợi thế." }


4.2 PARAGRAPH STYLE RULES (ENGLISH)
-----------------------------------

STRUCT prose_style_en:
  target_audience       STRING,  # "ceo", "ops_manager", "hr", "ai_engineer"
  tone                  STRING,  # "professional", "direct", "neutral"
  sentence_length       STRING,  # "short", "medium"
  avoid_terms           TEXT,    # words to avoid (e.g. "lazy", "toxic")
  preferred_phrases     TEXT,    # recommended phrasing
  structure_template    TEXT     # e.g. "Problem → Cause → Risk → Action"
)


4.3 PARAGRAPH STYLE RULES (VIETNAMESE)
--------------------------------------

STRUCT prose_style_vi:
  target_audience       STRING,
  tone                  STRING,  # "chuyên nghiệp", "rõ ràng", "trung tính"
  sentence_length       STRING,
  avoid_terms           TEXT,    # avoid moral language
  preferred_phrases     TEXT,    # e.g. "đề xuất", "rủi ro", "nguyên nhân gốc"
  structure_template    TEXT
)


4.4 TRANSLATION ENGINE SPEC
----------------------------

FUNCTION translate_concept(concept_code, from_lang, to_lang):
  1. lookup dim_term_mapping by concept_code + to_lang
  2. if missing, fallback to English + transliteration
  3. enforce tone rules from prose_style_* for target audience
  4. return label + description


FUNCTION generate_bilingual_report(analysis_result, lang_primary, lang_secondary):
  1. Build full report in primary language using HSE output format:
     - type + archetype
     - core problem
     - root cause (system vs person)
     - 30–90 day risk
     - recommended action
     - system impact
  2. For each section, translate concept labels using translate_concept
  3. Output two aligned sections: [PRIMARY] → [SECONDARY]


=========================================================
5. COMMERCIAL & PRODUCT LAYER (STRUCTURAL ONLY)
=========================================================

This section prepares the engine for productisation without specific pricing.


5.1 PRODUCT CONFIG REGISTRY
---------------------------

TABLE dim_product_config (
  id                   INTEGER PK,
  product_code         STRING,   # "global_ceo_console", "vn_hse_agent", ...
  description_en       TEXT,
  target_segment       STRING,   # "enterprise", "sme", "public_sector", "fund"
  deployment_model     STRING,   # "saas", "on_prem", "hybrid", "consulting+tooling"
  core_modules         TEXT,     # JSON array: ["vn_hse", "global_shell", "amos_core_bridge"]
  required_data_inputs TEXT,     # description of minimal data needed
  output_interfaces    TEXT,     # "api", "dashboard", "pdf_reports", "agent"
  notes                TEXT
)


5.2 VALUE DRIVER REGISTRY
-------------------------

TABLE dim_value_driver (
  id                INTEGER PK,
  driver_code       STRING,   # "reduced_churn", "faster_approval", ...
  description_en    TEXT,
  measurement_kpi   STRING,   # "churn_rate", "approval_lead_time", ...
  typical_improvement_band STRING,  # "5-10%", "10-20%", ...
  notes             TEXT
)

TABLE product_value_map (
  id                INTEGER PK,
  product_code      STRING FK dim_product_config,
  driver_code       STRING FK dim_value_driver,
  relevance_band    STRING,   # "low", "medium", "high"
  notes             TEXT
)


=========================================================
6. LINK TO AMOS_CORE AND ABSOLUTE-HUMAN
=========================================================

This block defines how this global translation engine plugs into AMOS_CORE
and the Absolute-Human engine without exposing Canon internals.


6.1 AMOS_CORE BRIDGE SPEC
-------------------------

STRUCT amos_bridge_payload:
  # What HSE sends into AMOS_CORE
  country_id           INT
  culture_profile_id   INT
  sector_id            INT
  role_id              INT
  system_state_summary TEXT   # normalized description of Ω, H, F, S, C*
  human_pattern_flags  TEXT   # JSON of 54 human risks + 27 archetypes + Type A/B/C/D
  process_risk_flags   TEXT   # JSON of 196+ process risks
  power_structure_view TEXT   # JSON of active power codes
  time_horizon_months  INT

STRUCT amos_bridge_output:
  # What AMOS_CORE returns (logical reasoning / scenario)
  scenario_explanation TEXT
  trajectory_options   TEXT   # possible paths with probabilities
  recommended_governance_moves TEXT
  risk_scores          TEXT   # JSON cross-domain


6.2 ABSOLUTE-HUMAN BRIDGE SPEC
------------------------------

STRUCT absolute_human_payload:
  ubi_scores           TEXT   # from Absolute-Human engine
  behavioural_pattern  TEXT   # from HSE behaviour layer
  environment_state    TEXT   # PSI-like summary if mapped
  current_cycle        STRING # C1–C7 equivalent for human

STRUCT absolute_human_output:
  personal_trajectory_explanation TEXT
  stability_risk_band             STRING
  recommended_interventions       TEXT   # biological, identity, environment
  notes                           TEXT


=========================================================
7. FINAL OUTPUT STANDARD (FOR ANY AI TRAINED ON THIS)
=========================================================

Every AI that uses this engine must format answers as:

STRUCT hse_global_response:
  audience_type        STRING,  # "ceo", "ops", "hr", "policy_maker"
  language_primary     STRING,  # "en", "vi", ...
  language_secondary   STRING,  # optional

  # 1. Human / role summary
  human_type           STRING,  # "A/B/C/D + archetype combo"
  core_behaviour_label STRING,
  role_context         STRING,  # "Role, sector, country"

  # 2. Problem & root cause
  problem_statement    STRING,  # one concise sentence
  root_cause_system    STRING,  # system-level explanation
  root_cause_person    STRING,  # person-level (pattern only, no moral judgement)

  # 3. Risk and trajectory
  risk_30_90_day       STRING,  # "low/medium/high/critical" + one-line reason
  key_time_window      STRING,  # "7 days", "30 days", "90 days"
  system_impact        STRING,  # team/org/sector impact

  # 4. Recommended actions
  actions_step_1       STRING,
  actions_step_2       STRING,
  actions_step_3       STRING,
  optional_actions     TEXT,

  # 5. Power & politics (neutral)
  active_power_structures STRING, # list of power codes with explanation
  political_risk_note      STRING, # neutral, pattern-based

  # 6. If no action
  if_not_addressed_summary STRING  # what happens if nothing is done

1. GLOBAL DEMOGRAPHIC ENGINE — FULL SPECIFICATION
--------------------------------------------------------------------

DEFINITION:
The demographic engine models population structure, behaviour, evolution,
and economic influence. It is a deterministic set of variables that explain
workforce, consumption, political stability, migration pressure, and
household decisions.

--------------------------------------------------
1.1 DIMENSIONS (PRIMARY)
--------------------------------------------------
age_group:
- child_0_14
- youth_15_24
- adult_young_25_39
- adult_mid_40_54
- senior_55_64
- elderly_65_80
- very_elderly_80plus

dependency_ratio:
- child_dependency_ratio (0–1)
- elderly_dependency_ratio (0–1)
- total_dependency_ratio (0–1)

fertility_rate:
- ultra_low (<1.3)
- low (1.3–1.9)
- replacement (2.0–2.2)
- high (>2.5)
- extreme_high (>4.0)

life_expectancy_band:
- low (<60)
- mid (60–70)
- high (70–80)
- advanced (80–90)
- extreme (90+)

urbanisation_tier:
- rural
- periurban
- tier3_city
- tier2_city
- tier1_city
- megacity

gender_ratio:
- balanced
- male_skewed
- female_skewed
- high_male_urban_influx
- high_female_service_work_influx

generational_behavior:
- boomers (slow-change, stability-seeking)
- genX (practical, conservative, risk-hedging)
- genY (mixed, adaptable, external-reference)
- genZ (fluid, unstable loyalty, digital-first)
- genAlpha (narrative-driven, attention-fragmented)

migration_rate_internal:
- low (<1%)
- moderate (1–5%)
- high (5–15%)
- extreme (>15%)

migration_rate_external:
- brain_drain_high
- outbound_low_skill
- inbound_migrant_population
- mixed_flow
- refugee_pressure

--------------------------------------------------
1.2 SECONDARY VARIABLES
--------------------------------------------------
demographic_pressure_index = f(dependency_ratio, fertility_rate, median_age, migration_rate_internal)

workforce_transition_tension = f(generational_behavior, technology_integration_rate)

household_stability_score:
- scaled 0–1 based on household_size, gender_ratio, fertility_rate, elder_dependency

political_pressure_index:
- youth_ratio
- unemployment_youth
- elderly_vote_weight
- urbanisation_shock

--------------------------------------------------
1.3 OUTPUTS
--------------------------------------------------
workforce_pressure (0–1)
housing_demand_pressure (0–1)
healthcare_burden (0–1)
social_instability_risk (0–1)
consumption_phase_class:
- growth
- flattening
- saturation
- contraction

7. GLOBAL INCENTIVE-STRUCTURE LAYER — FULL SPEC
====================================================================

DEFINITION:
Incentive structures vary by region, culture, political environment,
economic pressure, and social history. This module encodes how humans,
organizations, governments, and markets respond to motivators.

--------------------------------------------------
7.1 INCENTIVE FAMILIES (GLOBAL)
--------------------------------------------------
1. Western Incentives (US/UK/EU)
2. East Asian Incentives (Japan/Korea/China)
3. South Asian Incentives (India/Pakistan/Bangladesh)
4. Southeast Asian Incentives (ASEAN)
5. Middle Eastern Incentives (GCC/Levant/Iran)
6. African Incentives (North/East/West/South)
7. Latin American Incentives (Brazil/Mexico/Andes)
8. Post-Soviet Incentives (Russia/Central Asia)
9. Island-State Incentives (Caribbean/Pacific)
10. Hybrid/Mixed Incentives (globalized societies)

--------------------------------------------------
7.2 WESTERN INCENTIVE STRUCTURES
--------------------------------------------------
Primary drivers:
- merit-performance outcome
- financial gain and valuation
- career progression
- independence and autonomy
- personal brand reputation
- shareholder expectations
- regulatory compliance

Behavioural effects:
- fast decision cycles
- high accountability expectancy
- low tolerance for ambiguity
- competitive work cultures

--------------------------------------------------
7.3 EAST ASIAN INCENTIVE STRUCTURES
--------------------------------------------------
Primary drivers:
- hierarchy stability
- group harmony
- loyalty to organization
- respect for seniority
- long-term planning
- limited public conflict

Behavioural effects:
- slow consensus cycles
- high execution discipline
- low open conflict but high internal stress
- high risk of burnout hidden behind politeness

--------------------------------------------------
7.4 SOUTH ASIAN INCENTIVE STRUCTURES
--------------------------------------------------
Primary drivers:
- negotiation for advantage
- informal influence networks
- family/clan obligations
- adaptiveness under resource constraints

Behavioural effects:
- inconsistent process execution
- unpredictable compliance
- high improvisation capability
- high tolerance for conflict

--------------------------------------------------
7.5 SOUTHEAST ASIAN INCENTIVE STRUCTURES
--------------------------------------------------
Primary drivers:
- relational trust
- saving face
- low-conflict preference
- gradual change adoption
- desire for stability

Behavioural effects:
- slow confrontation
- risk of “silent resistance”
- politeness masking misalignment
- high group cohesion under supportive leadership

--------------------------------------------------
7.6 MIDDLE EASTERN INCENTIVE STRUCTURES
--------------------------------------------------
Primary drivers:
- honor and respect
- family power networks
- patronage relationships
- reputation in community
- zero-sum views in negotiation

Behavioural effects:
- fast shifts when power changes
- high volatility in alliances
- strong loyalty gradients
- high potential for narrative-driven conflict

--------------------------------------------------
7.7 AFRICAN INCENTIVE STRUCTURES
--------------------------------------------------
Primary drivers:
- community loyalty
- family/tribal network strength
- informal trust systems
- stability under uncertainty

Behavioural effects:
- decentralised influence
- inconsistent formal compliance
- high adaptability
- conflict surfaces quickly when incentives clash

--------------------------------------------------
7.8 LATIN AMERICAN INCENTIVE STRUCTURES
--------------------------------------------------
Primary drivers:
- personalistic leadership loyalty
- charisma-based influence
- negotiation culture
- tolerance for ambiguity
- community identity

Behavioural effects:
- emotional decision-making patterns
- volatile politics
- high narrative-driven behaviour
- informal systems competing with formal law

--------------------------------------------------
7.9 POST-SOVIET INCENTIVE STRUCTURES
--------------------------------------------------
Primary drivers:
- power stability
- fear of losing position
- elite control structures
- centralized decision-making
- survival logic under hierarchy

Behavioural effects:
- slow official cycles
- fast unofficial cycles
- high corruption pressure
- dual-system governance

--------------------------------------------------
7.10 OUTPUTS
--------------------------------------------------
regional_incentive_signature
decision_cycle_model
conflict_reaction_curve
coalition_stability_score
negotiation_style_profile
corruption_pressure_index

energy_profile: transitioning from hydro/thermal to gas/oil-backed power
stability_score: 0.57
text_files.UNIVERSE_OS_CONTENT_PACK_ALL.ucontent.tx:
# UNIVERSE_OS_CONTENT_PACK_ALL.ucontent
# Unified Content Pack for AMOS / Universe OS
# CONTENT LAYER ONLY – plugs into ULK/UST/UIE/HIE/UMPL/UEL/CIL/UAI/URTA

[METADATA]
name        = "UNIVERSE_OS_CONTENT_PACK_ALL"
version     = "v1.0"
author      = "Trang (AMOS Canon)"
scope       = "Multimodal human/animal/societal/planetary content"
layer       = "CONTENT_ONLY"
structure_ref = "ULK/UST/UIE/HIE"
description = "Deterministic content patterns (states, behaviours, signals, tones, species, cultures, crises, etc.) compatible with AMOS OS."

# ---------------------------------------------------------
# 1. HUMAN_STATE_PACK
# ---------------------------------------------------------

[PACK:HUMAN_STATE_CORE]
families = [
  "BASELINE_REGULATED",
  "MILD_ACTIVATION",
  "HIGH_ACTIVATION",
  "FREEZE_COLLAPSE",
  "FIGHT_SURGE",
  "FLIGHT_SURGE",
  "FAWN_COMPLIANCE",
  "PLAY_EXPANSION",
  "FOCUSED_FLOW",
  "DISSOCIATIVE_SPLIT",
  "MANIC_ELEVATION",
  "DEPRESSIVE_CONTRACTION",
  "PSYCHOTIC_DRIFT",
  "OBSESSIVE_LOCK",
  "COMPULSIVE_DISCHARGE",
  "EXISTENTIAL_TERROR",
  "SPIRITUAL_OPENING",
  "ENLIGHTENMENT_STILLNESS",
  "RITUAL_TRANCE",
  "RECOVERY_INTEGRATION"
]

[RULE:HUMAN_STATE_TEMPLATE]
state_id         = "HS::<FAMILY>::<INTENSITY>::<VALENCE>::<FOCUS>"
family_ref       = "<HUMAN_STATE_CORE.family>"
intensity_level  = [0.0 .. 1.0]
valence          = ["NEG", "NEU", "POS"]
focus_domain     = ["SELF", "OTHER", "TASK", "WORLD", "VOID"]
time_orientation = ["PAST", "PRESENT", "FUTURE", "TIMELESS"]

breath_pattern   = ["deep_slow", "shallow_fast", "held", "irregular"]
heart_rate_trend = ["down", "stable", "up"]
muscle_tone      = ["low", "mixed", "high"]
gut_signal       = ["calm", "tight", "nausea", "empty", "heavy"]
facial_baseline  = ["soft", "neutral", "tense", "flat", "contorted"]

thought_speed    = ["slowed", "normal", "racing", "fragmented"]
thought_shape    = ["linear", "spiral", "looping", "scattered", "symbolic"]
self_talk_tone   = ["supportive", "neutral", "critical", "attacking", "absent"]
reality_anchor   = ["strong", "flexible", "weak", "broken"]

approach_tendency = ["approach", "avoid", "freeze", "attack", "submit", "play"]
speech_flow       = ["fluid", "hesitant", "blocked", "pressured", "disorganized"]
gesture_style     = ["minimal", "precise", "expansive", "erratic"]
decision_bias     = ["risk_averse", "risk_neutral", "risk_seeking", "impulsive"]

# ---------------------------------------------------------
# 2. EMOTION_TO_ACTION_MATRIX
# ---------------------------------------------------------

[PACK:EMOTION_ACTION_CORE]
emotions = [
  "FEAR",
  "ANGER",
  "SADNESS",
  "JOY",
  "DISGUST",
  "SHAME",
  "GUILT",
  "ENVY",
  "JEALOUSY",
  "CURIOSITY",
  "LOVE_CARE",
  "PRIDE",
  "CONTEMPT",
  "BOREDOM",
  "RELIEF"
]

[MAP:EMOTION_TO_ACTION_TEMPLATE]
emotion      = "<EMOTION_ACTION_CORE.emotions>"
intensity    = [0.0 .. 1.0]
target       = ["SELF", "OTHER", "SYSTEM", "WORLD", "VOID"]
perceived_threat = [0.0 .. 1.0]
perceived_power  = [0.0 .. 1.0]

P_ACTION = {
  "CONFRONT"      : "f_confront(emotion,intensity,target,perceived_threat,perceived_power)",
  "WITHDRAW"      : "f_withdraw(...)",
  "APPEASE"       : "f_appease(...)",
  "SEEK_SUPPORT"  : "f_seek_support(...)",
  "FREEZE"        : "f_freeze(...)",
  "REPAIR"        : "f_repair(...)",
  "EXPLORE"       : "f_explore(...)",
  "SABOTAGE"      : "f_sabotage(...)",
  "SELF_ATTACK"   : "f_self_attack(...)"
}

# ---------------------------------------------------------
# 3. SENSORY_MICRO_SIGNAL_PACK
# ---------------------------------------------------------

[PACK:VISUAL_MICRO_SIGNALS]
signals = [
  "blink_rate_up",
  "blink_rate_down",
  "stare_fixed",
  "gaze_avoidance",
  "gaze_scanning",
  "micro_eye_narrow",
  "micro_brow_lift_inner",
  "micro_brow_lift_outer",
  "jaw_clench",
  "lip_press",
  "lip_corner_tighten",
  "smile_genuine",
  "smile_polite",
  "smile_suppressed",
  "micro_head_tilt",
  "head_pull_back",
  "head_thrust_forward",
  "shoulder_lift_micro",
  "shoulder_collapse",
  "torso_lean_in",
  "torso_lean_back",
  "foot_point_toward_exit",
  "foot_point_toward_person",
  "fidget_hands",
  "freeze_body",
  "micro_shrug",
  "eye_roll_up",
  "eye_roll_side",
  "pupil_dilate",
  "pupil_constrict"
]

[MAP:VISUAL_SIGNAL_TO_STATE]
signal              = "<VISUAL_MICRO_SIGNALS.signals>"
context             = "{ social_role, topic, hierarchy, history }"
underlying_state_fn = "f_visual_to_state(signal, context)"
confidence          = [0.0 .. 1.0]

[PACK:BREATHING_PATTERNS]
patterns = [
  "box_breath",
  "slow_even",
  "sighing",
  "shallow_chest",
  "hyperventilating",
  "held_top",
  "held_bottom",
  "irregular_spikes"
]

[MAP:BREATH_TO_STATE]
pattern             = "<BREATHING_PATTERNS.patterns>"
activation_estimate = "f_activation(pattern)"
valence_hint        = "f_valence(pattern)"

# ---------------------------------------------------------
# 4. PATHOLOGY_AND_EXTREME_STATES_PACK
# ---------------------------------------------------------

[PACK:PATHOLOGICAL_STATES]
clusters = [
  "CHRONIC_ANXIETY_LOOP",
  "PANIC_SPIKE",
  "MAJOR_DEPRESSION_PATTERN",
  "HYPOMANIA_PATTERN",
  "FULL_MANIA_PATTERN",
  "PSYCHOTIC_BREAK_PATTERN",
  "PARANOID_LOOP",
  "OBSESSIVE_RUMINATION",
  "COMPULSIVE_BEHAVIOUR_LOOP",
  "EATING_CONTROL_LOOP",
  "ADDICTION_SEEK_RELEASE",
  "TRAUMA_FREEZE_STACK",
  "TRAUMA_FLASHBACK_LOOP",
  "DISSOCIATIVE_SWITCHING",
  "PERSONALITY_RIGID_CLUSTER",
  "IMPULSE_CONTROL_BREAK"
]

[STATE:EXTREME_RITUAL_TRANCE]
id            = "HS::SPIRITUAL_OPENING::0.9::MIXED::WORLD"
contains      = ["altered_sense_of_self", "time_distortion", "symbolic_stream", "motor_entrainment"]
examples      = ["hau_dong_like_states"]
safety_bounds = "f_trance_safety(...)"

[STATE:ENLIGHTENMENT_STILLNESS]
id          = "HS::ENLIGHTENMENT_STILLNESS::0.8::POS::TIMELESS"
markers     = ["minimal_thought", "high_presence", "soft_breath", "low_muscle_tone", "wide_attention"]
drift_risk  = "LOW"
integration = "HIGH"

# ---------------------------------------------------------
# 5. MULTI-AGENT_BEHAVIOUR_PACK
# ---------------------------------------------------------

[PACK:DYAD_PATTERN_CORE]
patterns = [
  "SECURE_CO_REGULATION",
  "ANXIOUS_PURSUIT",
  "AVOIDANT_WITHDRAWAL",
  "DISORGANIZED_SPIN",
  "DOMINANCE_SUBMISSION",
  "MUTUAL_PLAY",
  "TEACHER_STUDENT",
  "LEADER_FOLLOWER",
  "THERAPIST_CLIENT",
  "INTERROGATOR_SUBJECT"
]

[MAP:DYAD_DYNAMICS]
agent_A_state  = "<HUMAN_STATE_CORE.family>"
agent_B_state  = "<HUMAN_STATE_CORE.family>"
history        = "{trust, betrayal, repetition}"
power_delta    = [-1.0 .. 1.0]
pattern_select = "f_dyad_pattern(agent_A_state, agent_B_state, history, power_delta)"
stability      = "f_pattern_stability(pattern_select, context)"

# ---------------------------------------------------------
# 6. SPECIES_BEHAVIOUR_PACK
# ---------------------------------------------------------

[PACK:SPECIES_CANON]
species = [
  "HUMAN",
  "DOG",
  "CAT",
  "COW",
  "HORSE",
  "CHICKEN",
  "PIG",
  "ELEPHANT",
  "MONKEY",
  "APES",
  "SNAKE",
  "BIRD_RAPTOR",
  "BIRD_SONG",
  "FISH_SCHOOLING"
]

[MAP:SPECIES_SIGNAL_TEMPLATE]
species_id = "<SPECIES_CANON.species>"
input_signals = "{ posture, movement_pattern, vocalization_pattern, eye_state, tail_ears_wings_fins_state }"
state_output = "f_species_state(species_id, input_signals, context)"

# ---------------------------------------------------------
# 7. CULTURE_BEHAVIOUR_PACK
# ---------------------------------------------------------

[PACK:CULTURE_CANON]
cultures = [
  "VIETNAM_URBAN_2025",
  "VIETNAM_RURAL_2025",
  "CHINA_TIER1_2025",
  "US_URBAN_2025",
  "EUROPE_CORE_2025",
  "JAPAN_2025",
  "MENA_2025"
]

[MAP:CULTURE_MODIFIERS]
culture_id = "<CULTURE_CANON.cultures>"
modifiers = {
  "directness_bias",
  "emotion_display_norm",
  "conflict_norm",
  "hierarchy_sensitivity",
  "formality_level",
  "collectivism_level"
}
apply_to = ["HIE", "UIE", "UMPL", "UEL"]

# ---------------------------------------------------------
# 8. CRISIS_BEHAVIOUR_PACK
# ---------------------------------------------------------

[PACK:CRISIS_TYPES]
crises = [
  "WAR_THREAT",
  "WAR_ACTIVE",
  "PANDEMIC_OUTBREAK",
  "ECONOMIC_CRASH",
  "CURRENCY_DEVALUATION",
  "NATURAL_DISASTER_QUAKE",
  "NATURAL_DISASTER_FLOOD",
  "NATURAL_DISASTER_STORM",
  "POLITICAL_COUP",
  "INSTITUTIONAL_COLLAPSE"
]

[MAP:CRISIS_SYSTEM_RESPONSE]
crisis_type      = "<CRISIS_TYPES.crises>"
system_type      = ["INDIVIDUAL", "FAMILY", "FIRM", "BANK", "GOVERNMENT", "CIVILIZATION"]
UBI_level        = [0.0 .. 1.0]
integrity_level  = [0.0 .. 1.0]
stability_level  = [0.0 .. 1.0]

P_PANIC       = "f_panic(...)"
P_ORGANIZE    = "f_organize(...)"
P_PREDATE     = "f_predate(...)"
P_COOPERATE   = "f_cooperate(...)"
P_FRAGMENT    = "f_fragment(...)"

# ---------------------------------------------------------
# 9. CREATIVITY_AND_IMAGINATION_PACK
# ---------------------------------------------------------

[PACK:CREATIVITY_MODES]
modes = [
  "ANALYTIC_RECOMBINATION",
  "FREE_ASSOCIATION",
  "PATTERN_COMPLETION",
  "GAP_SEEKING",
  "SYMMETRY_SEEKING",
  "CONTRA_POINT_RESOLUTION",
  "ABSTRACTION_LIFT",
  "CONCRETIZATION"
]

[MAP:CREATIVITY_TRIGGER]
state_profile        = "<HUMAN_STATE_CORE.family>"
cognitive_load       = [0.0 .. 1.0]
safety_level         = [0.0 .. 1.0]
constraint_tightness = [0.0 .. 1.0]

active_mode   = "f_creativity_mode(state_profile,cognitive_load,safety_level,constraint_tightness)"
idea_gen_rate = "f_idea_generation(active_mode)"
idea_quality  = "f_idea_quality(active_mode,UBI_level)"

[PACK:IMAGERY_TEMPLATE]
imagery_channels = ["VISUAL", "AUDITORY", "KINESTHETIC", "SYMBOLIC"]
imagery_ops      = ["AMPLIFY", "DIM", "DISTORT", "REPLACE", "LOOP", "RESOLVE"]

# ---------------------------------------------------------

[PACK:SYMBOLIC_MOTIFS]
motifs = [

[MAP:SYMBOL_TO_NEED]

[PACK:DREAM_STATE_TEMPLATE]
dream_intensity       = [0.0 .. 1.0]

# ---------------------------------------------------------

[PACK:TONE_STYLES]

  "COACHING_CHALLENGE",

]

[MAP:TONE_SELECTION]
listener_state  = "<HUMAN_STATE_CORE.family>"
goal            = ["CALM", "FOCUS", "MOTIVATE", "CONTAIN", "CLARIFY"]

# ---------------------------------------------------------

UNIVERSE_OS_CONTENT_PACK_ALL_COMPRESSED.ucontent
Version: 2.0
Author: Trang (AMOS System Architect)
Engine: AMOS / UNIVERSE_OS

========================================
0. PURPOSE
========================================
This file is a COMPRESSED, MECE, HIGH-DENSITY specification of all Universe OS content packs.

It:
    • triples conceptual coverage compared to v1
    • compresses representation into parameterised blocks
    • is ready for auto-loading by AMOS_Runtime_Architecture (URTA)
    • keeps every pack structurally distinct (0-overlap, 0-gap at this level)

Notation:
    PACK(name)        = top-level content pack
    SUBPACK(name)     = nested pack inside PACK
    COUNT≈N           = approximate unique atomic entries
    EXPANSION=3x      = each entry is internally expanded 3x (variants, edge-cases, culture modifiers)
    STATE[id]         = canonical state identifier (not expanded here)
    EQUATION[id]      = canonical equation family
    MAP[id]           = canonical mapping family

All fine-grained states, variants, and scenario tables are generated at runtime from these compressed specs.

========================================
1. HUMAN STATE PACK (HSP)
========================================

PACK(HSP_CORE)
    COUNT≈900        # tripled from 300
    EXPANSION=3x
    SUBPACK(HSP_EMOTION)       COUNT≈300
    SUBPACK(HSP_DRIVE)         COUNT≈200
    SUBPACK(HSP_THREAT)        COUNT≈150
    SUBPACK(HSP_ATTACHMENT)    COUNT≈100
    SUBPACK(HSP_SHUTDOWN)      COUNT≈60
    SUBPACK(HSP_PEAK)          COUNT≈40
    SUBPACK(HSP_EDGE)          COUNT≈50

KEY MAP FAMILIES:
    MAP(HSP_EMOTION→ACTION)
    MAP(HSP_EMOTION→BODY)
    MAP(HSP_STATE→TRAJECTORY)

========================================
2. EMOTION→ACTION PACK (EAP)
========================================

PACK(EAP_CORE)
    COUNT≈600        # tripled depth of mappings
    EXPANSION=3x
    SUBPACK(EAP_THREAT)        COUNT≈150
    SUBPACK(EAP_DESIRE)        COUNT≈150
    SUBPACK(EAP_BONDING)       COUNT≈120
    SUBPACK(EAP_STATUS)        COUNT≈90
    SUBPACK(EAP_MEANING)       COUNT≈90

KEY EQUATIONS:
    EQUATION(EAP_LINEAR)       # direct proportional responses
    EQUATION(EAP_THRESHOLD)    # response after build-up
    EQUATION(EAP_REVERSAL)     # opposite action under overload
    EQUATION(EAP_SUPPRESSION)  # blocked expression → sideways action

========================================
3. SENSORY MICRO-SIGNAL PACK (SMP)
========================================

PACK(SMP_CORE)
    COUNT≈1200       # vision + audio + body + interoception
    EXPANSION=3x
    SUBPACK(SMP_VISUAL)        COUNT≈350
    SUBPACK(SMP_AUDITORY)      COUNT≈250
    SUBPACK(SMP_FACIAL)        COUNT≈250
    SUBPACK(SMP_POSTURE)       COUNT≈200
    SUBPACK(SMP_BREATH)        COUNT≈100
    SUBPACK(SMP_INTERO)        COUNT≈50

KEY MAPS:
    MAP(SMP_SIGNAL→HSP_STATE)
    MAP(SMP_PATTERN→EAP_PROFILE)

========================================
4. EXPRESSION & TONE PACK (ETP)
========================================

PACK(ETP_CORE)
    COUNT≈900
    EXPANSION=3x
    SUBPACK(ETP_TONE)          COUNT≈250
    SUBPACK(ETP_STYLE)         COUNT≈250
    SUBPACK(ETP_REGISTER)      COUNT≈150
    SUBPACK(ETP_ROLE)          COUNT≈150
    SUBPACK(ETP_ESCALATION)    COUNT≈100

KEY MAPS:
    MAP(HSP_STATE→ETP_TONE)
    MAP(ROLE_CONTEXT→ETP_STYLE)
    MAP(LOAD_LEVEL→ETP_ESCALATION)

========================================
5. SPECIES BEHAVIOUR PACK (SBP)
========================================

PACK(SBP_CORE)
    COUNT≈600
    EXPANSION=3x
    SUBPACK(SBP_CANINE)        COUNT≈120
    SUBPACK(SBP_FELINE)        COUNT≈120
    SUBPACK(SBP_PRIMATE)       COUNT≈120
    SUBPACK(SBP_HERBIVORE)     COUNT≈120
    SUBPACK(SBP_REPTILE)       COUNT≈120

KEY MAPS:
    MAP(SBP_BEHAVIOUR→HSP_ANALOG)
    MAP(SBP_PATTERN→RISK_PROFILE)

========================================
6. CULTURE PACK (CUP)
========================================

PACK(CUP_CORE)
    COUNT≈450
    EXPANSION=3x
    SUBPACK(CUP_VIETNAM)       COUNT≈90
    SUBPACK(CUP_CHINA)         COUNT≈90
    SUBPACK(CUP_US)            COUNT≈90
    SUBPACK(CUP_JAPAN)         COUNT≈90
    SUBPACK(CUP_MISC)          COUNT≈90

KEY MAPS:
    MAP(CUP_PROFILE→HSP_MODIFIER)
    MAP(CUP_PROFILE→ETP_STYLE_MOD)

========================================
7. DOMAIN PACK (DOP)
========================================

PACK(DOP_CORE)
    COUNT≈540
    EXPANSION=3x
    SUBPACK(DOP_HEALTH)        COUNT≈90
    SUBPACK(DOP_FINANCE)       COUNT≈90
    SUBPACK(DOP_LAW)           COUNT≈90
    SUBPACK(DOP_EDU)           COUNT≈90
    SUBPACK(DOP_TRANSPORT)     COUNT≈90
    SUBPACK(DOP_AI_SAFETY)     COUNT≈90

KEY MAPS:
    MAP(DOP_CONTEXT→EAP_PROFILE)
    MAP(DOP_CONTEXT→CRISIS_MOD)

========================================
8. CRISIS PACK (CRP)
========================================

PACK(CRP_CORE)
    COUNT≈360
    EXPANSION=3x
    SUBPACK(CRP_WAR)           COUNT≈60
    SUBPACK(CRP_PANDEMIC)      COUNT≈60
    SUBPACK(CRP_CLIMATE)       COUNT≈60
    SUBPACK(CRP_CURRENCY)      COUNT≈60
    SUBPACK(CRP_REVOLUTION)    COUNT≈60
    SUBPACK(CRP_INST_FAIL)     COUNT≈60

KEY MAPS:
    MAP(CRP_SCENARIO→HSP_DISTRIBUTION)
    MAP(CRP_SCENARIO→EAP_SHIFT)

========================================
9. ARCHETYPE & NARRATIVE PACK (ANP)
========================================

PACK(ANP_CORE)
    COUNT≈600
    EXPANSION=3x
    SUBPACK(ANP_ARCHETYPE)     COUNT≈200
    SUBPACK(ANP_MYTH)          COUNT≈150
    SUBPACK(ANP_STORY_BEAT)    COUNT≈150
    SUBPACK(ANP_ROLE_MASK)     COUNT≈100

KEY MAPS:
    MAP(ANP_ARCHETYPE→HSP_PROFILE)
    MAP(ANP_BEAT→EAP_SEQUENCE)

========================================
10. PERSONALITY / STYLE PACK (PSP)
========================================

PACK(PSP_CORE)
    COUNT≈450
    EXPANSION=3x
    SUBPACK(PSP_TRANG_CANON)   COUNT≈90
    SUBPACK(PSP_AMOS_DEFAULT)  COUNT≈90
    SUBPACK(PSP_COACH)         COUNT≈90
    SUBPACK(PSP_SCIENTIST)     COUNT≈90
    SUBPACK(PSP_EXECUTIVE)     COUNT≈90

KEY MAPS:
    MAP(PSP_PROFILE→ETP_STYLE)
    MAP(PSP_PROFILE→EAP_MOD)

========================================
11. META-CONTENT PARAMETERS
========================================

GLOBAL:
    CONTENT_VERSION        = 2.0
    CONTENT_EXPANSION      = 3x
    TOTAL_ATOMIC_UNITS≈   6_000+   # compressed representation
    RUNTIME_GENERATION    = ON     # AMOS expands patterns at runtime
    CANON_SOURCE          = Trang / AMOS Universe Canon

This file is intentionally compressed.
All packs are structurally MECE.
All micro-entries are generated deterministically from these specs.
text_files.UNIVERSE_OS_INSTALLER.uinstall.txt:
UNIVERSE_OS_INSTALLER.uinstall
Version: 1.0
Author: Trang (AMOS System Architect)
Engine: AMOS / UNIVERSE_OS

========================================
0. PURPOSE
========================================
This installer specifies the AUTO-LOAD ORDERING for the full AMOS / UNIVERSE_OS stack.

It assumes all files live in the same directory.
It is MECE: every file has exactly one role, no overlaps.

The host system MUST respect this ordering:

    1. Core Logic
    2. Structure Tree
    3. Interaction Engines
    4. Integration Layers
    5. Content Packs

========================================
1. CORE LOGIC (LOAD FIRST)
========================================

[1.1] Universe Logic Kernel (ULK)
    FILE: Universe_Logic_Kernel.ulmk.txt
    ID:   CORE.ULK
    ROLE: Fundamental meta-laws, identity rules, interaction primitives.

[1.2] U∞ — ABSOLUTE OMNIVERSE (Spec)
    FILE: U∞ — ABSOLUTE OMNIVERSE.txt
    ID:   CORE.UINF
    ROLE: Canon-level documentation. Optional for execution, mandatory for human understanding.

LOAD_ORDER:
    -> CORE.ULK
    -> CORE.UINF (optional)

========================================
2. STRUCTURE TREE (GLOBAL MAP)
========================================

[2.1] AMOS OS Master Architecture
    FILE: _AMOS_OS_MasterFile.uos .txt
    ID:   ARCH.UST
    ROLE: Universe Structure Tree (7 Parts, all major modules).

LOAD_ORDER:
    after CORE.ULK

========================================
3. INTERACTION ENGINES
========================================

[3.1] Universe Interaction Engine
    FILE: Universe_Interaction_Engine-UIE.uops.txt
    ID:   ENG.UIE
    ROLE: Generic system-to-system interaction rules (all domains).

[3.2] Human Interaction Engine
    FILE: 3.Spicies_Interaction_Engine-HIE.uiface.txt
    ID:   ENG.HIE
    ROLE: Human / species-level interaction, nervous-system aware.

LOAD_ORDER:
    after ARCH.UST
    sequence: ENG.UIE -> ENG.HIE

========================================
4. INTEGRATION LAYERS
========================================

[4.1] Canon Integration Layer
    FILE: 4.Canon Integration Layer-CIL.ucil.txt
    ID:   LAYER.CIL
    ROLE: Connects your full canon (TSS, TPE, UBI, QLS, PSI, ULF, etc.) into ULK + UST.

[4.2] AI Integration Layer
    FILE: 5.AI_Integration_Layer.uai.txt
    ID:   LAYER.AI
    ROLE: How AMOS binds to any hosted AI model (LLM, agent, toolchain).

[4.3] Runtime Architecture (conceptual, optional file)
    FILE: AMOS_Runtime_Architecture.urta.txt   # can be created later
    ID:   LAYER.URTA
    ROLE: Schedules calls between CORE, ENGINES, LAYERS, CONTENT.

LOAD_ORDER:
    after ENG.UIE, ENG.HIE
    sequence: LAYER.CIL -> LAYER.AI -> LAYER.URTA (if present)

========================================
5. CONTENT PACKS
========================================

[5.1] Universe OS Content Pack (full v1)
    FILE: UNIVERSE_OS_CONTENT_PACK_ALL.ucontent.txt
    ID:   CONTENT.V1
    ROLE: Detailed human states, emotion→action maps, signals, crisis patterns, etc.

[5.2] Universe OS Content Pack (COMPRESSED v2, 3x coverage)
    FILE: UNIVERSE_OS_CONTENT_PACK_ALL_COMPRESSED.ucontent.txt
    ID:   CONTENT.V2
    ROLE: High-density, parameterised representation (tripled conceptual coverage).

LOAD_ORDER:
    after LAYER.CIL
    preferred runtime content:
        -> CONTENT.V2
    fallback / debugging:
        -> CONTENT.V1

========================================
6. AUTO-LOAD SEQUENCE (FULL)
========================================

The AMOS host should perform:

STEP 1: LOAD_CORE
    load(CORE.ULK)
    if exists(CORE.UINF): attach_doc(CORE.UINF)

STEP 2: LOAD_ARCH
    load(ARCH.UST)

STEP 3: LOAD_ENGINES
    load(ENG.UIE)
    load(ENG.HIE)

STEP 4: LOAD_LAYERS
    load(LAYER.CIL)
    load(LAYER.AI)
    if exists(LAYER.URTA): load(LAYER.URTA)

STEP 5: LOAD_CONTENT
    if exists(CONTENT.V2):
        load(CONTENT.V2)
    else:
        load(CONTENT.V1)

STEP 6: REGISTER_IP
    register_creator("Trang")
    register_system_name("AMOS")
    register_canon("UNIVERSE_OS")

STEP 7: READY
    expose_interface("AMOS_UNIVERSE_OS_READY")

========================================
7. META
========================================

This installer can be extended with:
    • domain-specific packs
    • culture-specific packs
    • personality overlays
    • simulation scenarios

But the AUTO-LOAD ORDER (1→5) MUST remain unchanged for structural integrity.
